- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

1	"""I-arch-001a — single helper for v6-mode manifest augmentation.
2	
3	Pipeline-A writes manifest.json at 6 sites (line refs in run_honest_sweep_r3.py:
4	1331/1453/1667/1746/2303/2806). When v6_mode is active, each call routes
5	through augment_v6_manifest() to add the v6 fields the API + run_store +
6	F-snowball bridge require. When v6_mode is False, only the I-gen-004 (#496)
7	``reasoning_trace`` reference is added (see augment_v6_manifest); all other
8	v6 fields stay absent.
9	"""
10	
11	from __future__ import annotations
12	
13	from typing import Any
14	
15	
16	def augment_v6_manifest(
17	    manifest: dict[str, Any],
18	    *,
19	    external_run_id: str | None,
20	    decision_id: str | None,
21	    query_slug: str | None,
22	    retrieval_block: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
23	    adequacy_block: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
24	    models_block: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
25	) -> dict[str, Any]:
26	    """Return augmented manifest dict. Does not mutate input.
27	
28	    The I-gen-004 (#496) ``reasoning_trace`` reference is added on EVERY
29	    invocation (operator transparency directive). Non-v6 invocations
30	    (external_run_id is None) otherwise get the input back unchanged — no
31	    v6 run_store / retrieval / adequacy / models fields.
32	    """
33	    augmented = dict(manifest)
34	    # I-gen-004 (#496): every run writes reasoning_trace.jsonl (the raw model
35	    # reasoning channel — process-transparency evidence, NOT verified claims;
36	    # strict_verify is never run against it). Referenced unconditionally so
37	    # an operator can locate it from any manifest — success / abort / error,
38	    # v6-mode or legacy CLI. Filename mirrors
39	    # generator.reasoning_trace.REASONING_TRACE_FILENAME.
40	    augmented["reasoning_trace"] = {
41	        "file": "reasoning_trace.jsonl",
42	        "description": (
43	            "raw model reasoning channel captured per generator LLM call; "
44	            "process-transparency evidence, NOT verified claims"
45	        ),
46	    }
47	    if external_run_id is None:
48	        return augmented
49	
50	    augmented["external_run_id"] = external_run_id
51	    augmented["query_slug"] = query_slug
52	
53	    scope = dict(augmented.get("scope") or {})
54	    scope["decision_id"] = decision_id
55	    augmented["scope"] = scope
56	
57	    if retrieval_block is not None:
58	        augmented["retrieval"] = retrieval_block
59	    if adequacy_block is not None:
60	        augmented["adequacy"] = adequacy_block
61	    if models_block is not None:
62	        augmented["models"] = models_block
63	
64	    return augmented
65

1	"""JobRunner abstraction + MockJobRunner.
2	
3	A JobRunner is a callable that executes a single job. It receives a
4	"control" object exposing pause-check + checkpoint persistence, so the
5	runner cooperatively yields when the queue requests pause or cancel.
6	
7	Concrete runners:
8	  - MockJobRunner: sleeps + checkpoints periodically. Used by tests
9	    and Phase A demo to validate the queue/worker plumbing without
10	    burning a full V30 sweep.
11	  - HonestSweepJobRunner (M-9): wires the actual V30 Phase-2 sweep.
12	"""
13	
14	from __future__ import annotations
15	
16	import time
17	from abc import ABC, abstractmethod
18	from dataclasses import dataclass
19	from typing import Any, Callable, Mapping
20	
21	from src.polaris_graph.audit_ir.job_queue import Job, JobQueue, JobQueueError
22	
23	
24	class JobControl:
25	    """Cooperative-yield control surface passed to JobRunner.run().
26	
27	    The runner must call `checkpoint(...)` periodically. checkpoint()
28	    persists progress to the queue AND raises a sentinel if pause/cancel
29	    was requested.
30	    """
31	
32	    class Cancelled(Exception):
33	        """Raised inside runner code when cancel was requested."""
34	
35	    class Paused(Exception):
36	        """Raised inside runner code when pause was requested."""
37	
38	    def __init__(self, queue: JobQueue, job_id: str) -> None:
39	        self._queue = queue
40	        self._job_id = job_id
41	
42	    def checkpoint(
43	        self,
44	        progress_pct: float,
45	        message: str = "",
46	        state: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None,
47	    ) -> None:
48	        """Persist progress and check for pause/cancel requests.
49	
50	        Raises JobControl.Cancelled or JobControl.Paused if the queue
51	        signals either. The runner should let the exception propagate;
52	        the worker handler converts it to the right queue state.
53	        """
54	        # Persist first so we don't lose progress on cooperative-yield
55	        # exceptions.
56	        self._queue.record_progress(
57	            self._job_id,
58	            progress_pct=progress_pct,
59	            progress_message=message,
60	            checkpoint=state,
61	        )
62	        # Then check for control-flag requests.
63	        job = self._queue.get(self._job_id)
64	        if job is None:
65	            raise JobQueueError(f"checkpoint: job {self._job_id} disappeared")
66	        if job.cancel_requested:
67	            raise JobControl.Cancelled()
68	        if job.pause_requested:
69	            raise JobControl.Paused()
70	
71	
72	class JobRunner(ABC):
73	    """Pluggable executor for one job type."""
74	
75	    template_id: str  # set by concrete subclasses
76	
77	    @abstractmethod
78	    def run(self, job: Job, control: JobControl) -> str | None:
79	        """Execute the job. Return the artifact_dir path on success.
80	
81	        Implementations should call control.checkpoint(...) at least once
82	        per significant step so pause/cancel requests are honored within
83	        a reasonable time bound.
84	        """
85	
86	
87	@dataclass
88	class MockJobRunner(JobRunner):
89	    """Test runner: sleeps total_seconds, checkpointing every step_seconds."""
90	
91	    template_id: str = "mock"
92	    total_seconds: float = 5.0
93	    step_seconds: float = 0.5
94	    artifact_dir: str | None = None
95	
96	    def run(self, job: Job, control: JobControl) -> str | None:
97	        steps = max(1, int(self.total_seconds / self.step_seconds))
98	        for i in range(steps):
99	            pct = (i + 1) / steps * 100.0
100	            control.checkpoint(
101	                progress_pct=pct,
102	                message=f"mock step {i + 1}/{steps}",
103	                state={"step": i + 1, "of": steps},
104	            )
105	            time.sleep(self.step_seconds)
106	        return self.artifact_dir
107	
108	
109	# Registry of runners, keyed by template_id. M-9 / M-10 register V30 + clinical
110	# templates here.
111	_RUNNERS: dict[str, JobRunner] = {}
112	
113	
114	def register_runner(runner: JobRunner) -> None:
115	    if not runner.template_id:
116	        raise ValueError("register_runner: runner.template_id required")
117	    _RUNNERS[runner.template_id] = runner
118	
119	
120	def get_runner(template_id: str) -> JobRunner | None:
121	    return _RUNNERS.get(template_id)
122	
123	
124	def list_runners() -> list[str]:
125	    return sorted(_RUNNERS.keys())
126	
127	
128	def _reset_runners_for_tests() -> None:
129	    _RUNNERS.clear()
130	    # Reset the inspector_router's deterministic-registration flag so the
131	    # next get_job_queue() call re-registers default runners.
132	    try:
133	        from src.polaris_graph.audit_ir import inspector_router as _ir
134	        _ir._runners_registered = False
135	    except ImportError:
136	        pass
137

1	"""JobWorker: background thread that pulls pending jobs and runs them.
2	
3	Per FINAL_PLAN.md: Phase B keeps a small in-process worker pool that
4	polls the JobQueue, claims jobs atomically, dispatches to the right
5	JobRunner, and converts cooperative-yield exceptions
6	(JobControl.Cancelled / JobControl.Paused) into queue state transitions.
7	
8	Phase B starts with one worker thread; Phase C scales horizontally
9	once the worker pool talks to a shared SQLite/Postgres store.
10	"""
11	
12	from __future__ import annotations
13	
14	import logging
15	import threading
16	import time
17	from typing import Optional
18	
19	from src.polaris_graph.audit_ir.job_queue import Job, JobQueue, JobQueueError
20	from src.polaris_graph.audit_ir.job_runner import JobControl, get_runner
21	
22	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
23	
24	
25	class JobWorker:
26	    """Single-thread worker that drains the queue.
27	
28	    Usage:
29	        worker = JobWorker(queue, poll_interval_s=1.0)
30	        worker.start()
31	        # ... later
32	        worker.stop()
33	    """
34	
35	    def __init__(
36	        self,
37	        queue: JobQueue,
38	        poll_interval_s: float = 1.0,
39	        on_job_completed: Optional[callable] = None,
40	    ) -> None:
41	        self._queue = queue
42	        self._poll_interval = poll_interval_s
43	        self._on_job_completed = on_job_completed
44	        self._stop_event = threading.Event()
45	        self._thread: Optional[threading.Thread] = None
46	
47	    def start(self) -> None:
48	        if self._thread is not None and self._thread.is_alive():
49	            return
50	        self._stop_event.clear()
51	        self._thread = threading.Thread(target=self._run_loop, daemon=True, name="polaris-job-worker")
52	        self._thread.start()
53	
54	    def stop(self, join_timeout: float = 10.0) -> None:
55	        self._stop_event.set()
56	        if self._thread is not None:
57	            self._thread.join(timeout=join_timeout)
58	
59	    def is_alive(self) -> bool:
60	        return self._thread is not None and self._thread.is_alive()
61	
62	    def run_one(self) -> Job | None:
63	        """Synchronous single-job drain. Used by tests."""
64	        job = self._queue.claim_pending()
65	        if job is None:
66	            return None
67	        return self._dispatch(job)
68	
69	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
70	    # Internals
71	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
72	
73	    def _run_loop(self) -> None:
74	        while not self._stop_event.is_set():
75	            try:
76	                job = self._queue.claim_pending()
77	            except JobQueueError as exc:
78	                logger.warning("worker: claim_pending failed: %s", exc)
79	                self._stop_event.wait(self._poll_interval)
80	                continue
81	            if job is None:
82	                # No work; sleep and poll again.
83	                self._stop_event.wait(self._poll_interval)
84	                continue
85	            try:
86	                self._dispatch(job)
87	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — last-resort safety
88	                logger.exception("worker: unhandled exception on job %s: %s", job.job_id, exc)
89	
90	    def _dispatch(self, job: Job) -> Job:
91	        runner = get_runner(job.template_id)
92	        if runner is None:
93	            return self._queue.mark_failed(
94	                job.job_id,
95	                error=f"no runner registered for template_id={job.template_id!r}",
96	            )
97	        control = JobControl(self._queue, job.job_id)
98	        try:
99	            artifact_dir = runner.run(job, control)
100	        except JobControl.Cancelled:
101	            logger.info("worker: job %s cancelled cooperatively", job.job_id)
102	            result = self._queue.mark_cancelled(job.job_id)
103	            self._prune_surfaces(job.job_id)
104	            if self._on_job_completed:
105	                self._on_job_completed(result)
106	            return result
107	        except JobControl.Paused:
108	            logger.info("worker: job %s paused cooperatively", job.job_id)
109	            return self._queue.mark_paused(job.job_id)
110	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
111	            logger.exception("worker: job %s failed: %s", job.job_id, exc)
112	            result = self._queue.mark_failed(
113	                job.job_id,
114	                error=f"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}",
115	            )
116	            self._prune_surfaces(job.job_id)
117	            if self._on_job_completed:
118	                self._on_job_completed(result)
119	            return result
120	        result = self._queue.mark_completed(job.job_id, artifact_dir=artifact_dir)
121	        self._prune_surfaces(job.job_id)
122	        if self._on_job_completed:
123	            self._on_job_completed(result)
124	        return result
125	
126	    @staticmethod
127	    def _prune_surfaces(job_id: str) -> None:
128	        """Codex M-13: drop progressive surface state for a
129	        terminal job. SSE subscribers receive a sentinel via
130	        bus.prune() so their event-stream loops exit cleanly.
131	        Best-effort — never let a bus error fail the worker."""
132	        try:
133	            from src.polaris_graph.audit_ir.progress_surfaces import (
134	                get_surface_bus,
135	            )
136	            get_surface_bus().prune(job_id)
137	        except Exception:  # pragma: no cover - defensive
138	            logger.exception("surface prune failed for job %s", job_id)
139

1	"""M-D10 (Phase D): Citation freshness monitoring — bootstrap.
2	
3	Per FINAL_PLAN M-D10 + Phase D milestones plan: detect when
4	cited DOIs/PMIDs are retracted, expressions of concern issued,
5	or guidance documents superseded. Pairs with M-D7's
6	`RetrievalCacheStore.evict_by_url` so that detected stale
7	sources flush from cache automatically.
8	
9	Phase 1 ships **detector contract + alerts substrate**:
10	  - FreshnessDetector protocol (caller injects real Crossref /
11	    PubMed / retraction-watch implementations)
12	  - FreshnessAlertStore extending M-21 SQLite substrate (one
13	    extra `freshness_alerts` table)
14	  - check_freshness() coordinator: runs detector, records the
15	    check, optionally evicts from M-D7 cache
16	
17	Phase 2 (deferred):
18	  - Real Crossref `update-policy` integration
19	  - Real PubMed retraction-status integration
20	  - Polling daemon (watches a workspace's cited URLs on a
21	    schedule)
22	  - Operator notification callbacks (writes into M-23 review
23	    queue when alert lands)
24	  - Empirical alert-latency ≤ 24h acceptance test
25	
26	## Status taxonomy
27	
28	The detector returns one of four discrete outcomes:
29	
30	  - `unchanged`: source still authoritative; no alert needed.
31	    Recorded as a heartbeat but doesn't trigger eviction.
32	  - `superseded`: a newer canonical version exists (e.g.
33	    Crossref `update-policy` indicates an updated DOI). Alert
34	    + evict cache.
35	  - `retracted`: source has been formally retracted or has an
36	    expression of concern. Alert + evict cache. Operator
37	    review required for any audit referencing this source.
38	  - `unreachable`: source returned 4xx/5xx or timed out. Alert
39	    + DO NOT evict cache (a temporary outage shouldn't drop a
40	    valid cached payload). Phase 2 daemon would retry-with-
41	    backoff before treating as superseded/retracted.
42	
43	## Why pluggable detector
44	
45	The real detection logic depends on source-specific protocols
46	(Crossref, PubMed, retraction watch). Phase 1 establishes the
47	contract so phase 2 can plug in concrete implementations
48	without rewriting the alert-recording substrate.
49	
50	For testing, callers inject a deterministic stub detector. For
51	production, callers will inject a real fetcher-backed detector
52	in phase 2.
53	
54	## Pin coupling
55	
56	Freshness alert state is workspace-scoped, NOT in
57	`ModelPin.retrieval_source_versions` (which is run-scoped).
58	Same boundary as M-D7's cache state. See
59	`docs/md10_phase1_threat_model.md` for the full boundary.
60	
61	## What "alert" means in phase 1
62	
63	Record-only. A FreshnessAlert lands in the
64	`freshness_alerts` table and is queryable via
65	`list_alerts(workspace_id, status=...)`. Phase 2 adds the
66	notification callback into M-23 review queue.
67	"""
68	
69	from __future__ import annotations
70	
71	import sqlite3
72	import time
73	import uuid
74	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
75	from enum import Enum
76	from pathlib import Path
77	from typing import Any, Callable, Protocol
78	
79	
80	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
81	# Errors
82	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
83	
84	
85	class FreshnessMonitorError(Exception):
86	    """Raised on schema/state violations."""
87	
88	
89	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
90	# Status taxonomy
91	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
92	
93	
94	class FreshnessStatus(Enum):
95	    """Closed taxonomy of detector verdicts.
96	
97	    `unchanged`: source still authoritative; alert recorded as
98	       heartbeat but no eviction.
99	    `superseded`: newer canonical version exists; alert +
100	       evict.
101	    `retracted`: formal retraction (Crossref `update-policy`
102	       retraction, PubMed `Retracted Publication`); alert +
103	       evict; operator review required.
104	    `expression_of_concern`: ICMJE/COPE expression of concern
105	       — the source's authority is in question but not formally
106	       retracted. Round-1 fix: split out from `retracted` so
107	       phase-2 operator workflows can route them differently
108	       (an EoC may be lifted; a retraction is permanent).
109	       Alert + evict — until the EoC is resolved, the cached
110	       payload should not be relied on.
111	    `unreachable`: 4xx/5xx or timeout; alert recorded but cache
112	       NOT evicted (transient outage doesn't justify dropping
113	       a valid cached payload). Phase 2 daemon will
114	       retry-with-backoff. Persistent unreachable does NOT
115	       promote to superseded — transport failure is not a
116	       content-freshness signal.
117	    """
118	
119	    UNCHANGED = "unchanged"
120	    SUPERSEDED = "superseded"
121	    RETRACTED = "retracted"
122	    EXPRESSION_OF_CONCERN = "expression_of_concern"
123	    UNREACHABLE = "unreachable"
124	
125	
126	# Statuses that trigger cache eviction.
127	_EVICTING_STATUSES = frozenset({
128	    FreshnessStatus.SUPERSEDED,
129	    FreshnessStatus.RETRACTED,
130	    FreshnessStatus.EXPRESSION_OF_CONCERN,
131	})

224	
225	_SCHEMA = """
226	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS freshness_alerts (
227	    alert_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
228	    workspace_id TEXT NOT NULL,
229	    source_url TEXT NOT NULL,
230	    cache_key TEXT NOT NULL,
231	    status TEXT NOT NULL,
232	    details TEXT,
233	    new_canonical_url TEXT,
234	    fetched_status_code INTEGER,
235	    checked_at REAL NOT NULL,
236	    evicted_cache_key TEXT,
237	    CHECK (status IN ('unchanged', 'superseded', 'retracted',
238	                      'expression_of_concern', 'unreachable'))
239	);
240	
241	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_freshness_ws_checked
242	    ON freshness_alerts(workspace_id, checked_at DESC);
243	
244	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_freshness_ws_status
245	    ON freshness_alerts(workspace_id, status);
246	
247	-- Latest-per-source queries key on the canonical cache_key,
248	-- not the raw source_url, so equivalent URL forms
249	-- (`10.1000/foo` vs `https://doi.org/10.1000/foo`) merge into
250	-- one history. Round-1 fix.
251	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_freshness_ws_cachekey_checked
252	    ON freshness_alerts(workspace_id, cache_key, checked_at DESC);
253	"""

260	
261	class FreshnessAlertStore:
262	    """SQLite-backed alert log, per-workspace isolated.
263	
264	    Pattern matches M-21 / M-D7: per-call WAL connections from a
265	    Path. Cross-workspace isolation: every read/write requires
266	    workspace_id; same DB file as workspace_memory + retrieval
267	    cache.
268	    """
269	
270	    def __init__(self, db_path: Path | str) -> None:
271	        self._db_path = Path(db_path)
272	        self._db_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
273	        self._init_schema()
274	
275	    def _connect(self) -> sqlite3.Connection:
276	        conn = sqlite3.connect(
277	            self._db_path, isolation_level=None, timeout=30.0,
278	        )
279	        conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
280	        conn.execute("PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL")
281	        conn.execute("PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON")
282	        return conn
283	
284	    def _init_schema(self) -> None:
285	        """Idempotent schema init with v1->v2 upgrade path.
286	
287	        v1 (commit 7bece98) had no `cache_key` column. v2
288	        (this build) requires it. If an existing v1 table is
289	        detected, ALTER TABLE adds the column and backfills
290	        canonical keys from `source_url`. The CHECK
291	        constraint on `status` was also extended (added
292	        `expression_of_concern`); SQLite can't ALTER an
293	        existing CHECK in place, so when the upgrade fires
294	        we rebuild the table via the standard SQLite recipe
295	        (rename old, create new, copy, drop). Idempotent —
296	        running twice is a no-op."""
297	        with self._connect() as conn:
298	            self._upgrade_v1_to_v2(conn)
299	            conn.executescript(_SCHEMA)
300	
301	    @staticmethod
302	    def _upgrade_v1_to_v2(conn: sqlite3.Connection) -> None:
303	        """Detect v1 schema and migrate to v2 in place.
304	
305	        v1 fingerprint: `freshness_alerts` table exists AND
306	        has no `cache_key` column. If both conditions hold,
307	        we rebuild via temp table to update the CHECK
308	        constraint AND add the new column with backfilled
309	        canonical keys."""
310	        cur = conn.execute(
311	            "SELECT name FROM sqlite_master "
312	            "WHERE type='table' AND name='freshness_alerts'"
313	        )
314	        if cur.fetchone() is None:
315	            return  # No table yet — fresh DB, full schema will create v2.
316	
317	        cur = conn.execute("PRAGMA table_info(freshness_alerts)")
318	        cols = {row[1] for row in cur.fetchall()}
319	        if "cache_key" in cols:
320	            return  # Already v2 (or newer).
321	
322	        # v1 detected. Migrate.
323	        from src.polaris_graph.audit_ir.retrieval_cache import (
324	            make_cache_key,
325	        )
326	
327	        # Rename old table.
328	        conn.execute(
329	            "ALTER TABLE freshness_alerts RENAME TO "
330	            "freshness_alerts_v1_old"
331	        )
332	        # Drop old indexes — they'd block recreate.
333	        for idx in (
334	            "idx_freshness_ws_checked",
335	            "idx_freshness_ws_status",
336	            "idx_freshness_ws_url_checked",
337	        ):
338	            conn.execute(f"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS {idx}")
339	        # Create the v2 table + indexes.
340	        conn.executescript(_SCHEMA)
341	        # Backfill: walk old rows, compute cache_key from
342	        # source_url, re-insert into v2 table.
343	        old_cur = conn.execute(
344	            "SELECT alert_id, workspace_id, source_url, status, "
345	            "details, new_canonical_url, fetched_status_code, "
346	            "checked_at, evicted_cache_key "
347	            "FROM freshness_alerts_v1_old"
348	        )
349	        for row in old_cur.fetchall():
350	            try:
351	                ck = make_cache_key(row[2])
352	            except Exception:
353	                # If a v1 row's source_url won't canonicalize,
354	                # we can't safely migrate it. Log to the row
355	                # itself via cache_key=`migration_failed:<url>`
356	                # so operators can see and triage. Fail-loud
357	                # over data loss.
358	                ck = f"migration_failed:{row[2][:200]}"
359	            conn.execute(
360	                "INSERT INTO freshness_alerts "
361	                "(alert_id, workspace_id, source_url, cache_key, "
362	                " status, details, new_canonical_url, "
363	                " fetched_status_code, checked_at, "
364	                " evicted_cache_key) "
365	                "VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)",
366	                (row[0], row[1], row[2], ck, row[3], row[4],
367	                 row[5], row[6], row[7], row[8]),
368	            )
369	        conn.execute("DROP TABLE freshness_alerts_v1_old")
370	
371	    def record(
372	        self,
373	        *,
374	        workspace_id: str,
375	        source_url: str,
376	        status: FreshnessStatus,
377	        details: str | None,
378	        new_canonical_url: str | None,
379	        fetched_status_code: int | None,
380	        checked_at: float,
381	        evicted_cache_key: str | None,
382	        cache_key: str | None = None,
383	    ) -> FreshnessAlert:
384	        """Persist one alert. Returns the saved record.
385	
386	        `cache_key` is computed from `source_url` if not
387	        provided. Round-1 fix: alerts dedup on canonical key,
388	        not raw URL — equivalent forms (`10.1000/foo` vs
389	        `https://doi.org/10.1000/foo`) become one history.
390	        """
391	        if not workspace_id or not workspace_id.strip():
392	            raise FreshnessMonitorError(
393	                "workspace_id must be non-empty"
394	            )
395	        if not source_url or not source_url.strip():
396	            raise FreshnessMonitorError(
397	                "source_url must be non-empty"
398	            )
399	        if not isinstance(status, FreshnessStatus):
400	            raise FreshnessMonitorError(
401	                f"status must be FreshnessStatus, "
402	                f"got {type(status).__name__}"
403	            )
404	        ws = workspace_id.strip()
405	        url = source_url.strip()
406	        alert_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
407	
408	        if cache_key is None:
409	            from src.polaris_graph.audit_ir.retrieval_cache import (
410	                make_cache_key,
411	            )
412	            try:
413	                cache_key = make_cache_key(url)
414	            except Exception as exc:
415	                raise FreshnessMonitorError(
416	                    f"could not canonicalize source_url for "
417	                    f"cache_key: {url!r}"
418	                ) from exc
419	        ck = cache_key.strip()
420	        if not ck:
421	            raise FreshnessMonitorError(
422	                "cache_key must be non-empty"
423	            )
424	
425	        with self._connect() as conn:
426	            conn.execute(
427	                """
428	                INSERT INTO freshness_alerts
429	                    (alert_id, workspace_id, source_url, cache_key,
430	                     status, details, new_canonical_url,
431	                     fetched_status_code, checked_at,
432	                     evicted_cache_key)
433	                VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
434	                """,
435	                (alert_id, ws, url, ck, status.value, details,
436	                 new_canonical_url, fetched_status_code,
437	                 checked_at, evicted_cache_key),
438	            )
439	        return FreshnessAlert(
440	            alert_id=alert_id,
441	            workspace_id=ws,
442	            source_url=url,
443	            cache_key=ck,
444	            status=status.value,
445	            details=details,
446	            new_canonical_url=new_canonical_url,
447	            fetched_status_code=fetched_status_code,
448	            checked_at=checked_at,
449	            evicted_cache_key=evicted_cache_key,
450	        )

452	    def list_alerts(
453	        self,
454	        *,
455	        workspace_id: str,
456	        status: FreshnessStatus | None = None,
457	        limit: int = 100,
458	    ) -> list[FreshnessAlert]:
459	        """Query alerts for a workspace, newest first.
460	
461	        `status` filter narrows to one taxonomy value.
462	        `limit` defaults to 100; pass a larger value if needed.
463	        Cross-workspace isolation: only returns rows for the
464	        passed workspace_id.
465	        """
466	        if not workspace_id or not workspace_id.strip():
467	            raise FreshnessMonitorError(
468	                "workspace_id must be non-empty"
469	            )
470	        if limit <= 0:
471	            raise FreshnessMonitorError("limit must be >0")
472	
473	        sql = (
474	            "SELECT * FROM freshness_alerts "
475	            "WHERE workspace_id=? "
476	        )
477	        params: list[Any] = [workspace_id.strip()]
478	        if status is not None:
479	            if not isinstance(status, FreshnessStatus):
480	                raise FreshnessMonitorError(
481	                    "status filter must be FreshnessStatus"
482	                )
483	            sql += "AND status=? "
484	            params.append(status.value)
485	        sql += "ORDER BY checked_at DESC LIMIT ?"
486	        params.append(limit)
487	
488	        with self._connect() as conn:
489	            cur = conn.execute(sql, params)
490	            rows = cur.fetchall()
491	        return [_row_to_alert(r) for r in rows]
492	
493	    def latest_for_url(
494	        self, workspace_id: str, source_url: str
495	    ) -> FreshnessAlert | None:
496	        """Newest alert for one source in this workspace.
497	
498	        Round-1 fix: queries by canonical cache_key, not raw
499	        source_url, so equivalent forms (`10.1000/foo`,
500	        `https://doi.org/10.1000/foo`, `?utm_source=x`) merge
501	        into one history. The alert's `source_url` field still
502	        preserves whichever form was originally cited.
503	
504	        Returns None if no alerts recorded for this canonical
505	        identity.
506	        """
507	        if not workspace_id or not workspace_id.strip():
508	            raise FreshnessMonitorError(
509	                "workspace_id must be non-empty"
510	            )
511	        if not source_url or not source_url.strip():
512	            raise FreshnessMonitorError(
513	                "source_url must be non-empty"
514	            )
515	        from src.polaris_graph.audit_ir.retrieval_cache import (
516	            make_cache_key,
517	        )
518	        try:
519	            ck = make_cache_key(source_url)
520	        except Exception as exc:
521	            raise FreshnessMonitorError(
522	                f"could not canonicalize source_url: {source_url!r}"
523	            ) from exc
524	        with self._connect() as conn:
525	            cur = conn.execute(
526	                "SELECT * FROM freshness_alerts "
527	                "WHERE workspace_id=? AND cache_key=? "
528	                "ORDER BY checked_at DESC LIMIT 1",
529	                (workspace_id.strip(), ck),
530	            )
531	            row = cur.fetchone()
532	        if row is None:
533	            return None
534	        return _row_to_alert(row)
535	
536	    def count(
537	        self,
538	        *,
539	        workspace_id: str,
540	        status: FreshnessStatus | None = None,
541	    ) -> int:
542	        """Total alerts (optionally filtered by status)."""
543	        if not workspace_id or not workspace_id.strip():
544	            raise FreshnessMonitorError(
545	                "workspace_id must be non-empty"
546	            )
547	        sql = (
548	            "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM freshness_alerts "
549	            "WHERE workspace_id=?"
550	        )
551	        params: list[Any] = [workspace_id.strip()]
552	        if status is not None:
553	            if not isinstance(status, FreshnessStatus):
554	                raise FreshnessMonitorError(
555	                    "status filter must be FreshnessStatus"
556	                )
557	            sql += " AND status=?"
558	            params.append(status.value)
559	        with self._connect() as conn:
560	            cur = conn.execute(sql, params)
561	            return cur.fetchone()[0]

564	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
565	# Coordinator
566	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
567	
568	
569	def check_freshness(
570	    *,
571	    workspace_id: str,
572	    source_url: str,
573	    detector: FreshnessDetector,
574	    store: FreshnessAlertStore,
575	    cache: Any | None = None,
576	    clock: Callable[[], float] = time.time,
577	) -> FreshnessAlert:
578	    """Run one freshness check end-to-end.
579	
580	    Steps:
581	      1. detector.detect(source_url) → FreshnessCheckResult
582	      2. If result.status in {SUPERSEDED, RETRACTED} AND cache
583	         provided, call cache.evict_by_url(workspace_id, url).
584	         Capture the cache_key that was evicted (if any).
585	      3. store.record(...) the alert with the eviction outcome.
586	      4. Return the persisted FreshnessAlert.
587	
588	    `cache` is `RetrievalCacheStore | None`; typed as Any here
589	    to avoid a hard import-time coupling — phase 1 can be tested
590	    with a pure stub.
591	
592	    `clock` is a `() -> float` callable. Tests inject a fixed
593	    clock to assert alert-latency semantics deterministically.
594	    """
595	    if not workspace_id or not workspace_id.strip():
596	        raise FreshnessMonitorError("workspace_id must be non-empty")
597	    if not source_url or not source_url.strip():
598	        raise FreshnessMonitorError("source_url must be non-empty")
599	    if detector is None:
600	        raise FreshnessMonitorError("detector must be provided")
601	    if store is None:
602	        raise FreshnessMonitorError("store must be provided")
603	
604	    result = detector.detect(source_url)
605	    if not isinstance(result, FreshnessCheckResult):
606	        raise FreshnessMonitorError(
607	            f"detector must return FreshnessCheckResult, "
608	            f"got {type(result).__name__}"
609	        )
610	
611	    # Compute canonical cache_key once — needed for eviction
612	    # AND for alert dedup. Failure to canonicalize is fatal:
613	    # we'd be unable to record a queryable alert OR evict.
614	    from src.polaris_graph.audit_ir.retrieval_cache import (
615	        make_cache_key,
616	    )
617	    try:
618	        cache_key = make_cache_key(source_url)
619	    except Exception as exc:
620	        raise FreshnessMonitorError(
621	            f"could not canonicalize source_url: {source_url!r}"
622	        ) from exc
623	
624	    evicted_key: str | None = None
625	    if cache is not None and result.status in _EVICTING_STATUSES:
626	        try:
627	            if cache.evict_by_url(workspace_id, source_url):
628	                evicted_key = cache_key
629	        except Exception:
630	            # Eviction failure must not silently swallow the
631	            # alert; re-raise so the caller knows the cache
632	            # is in an unknown state.
633	            raise
634	
635	    return store.record(
636	        workspace_id=workspace_id,
637	        source_url=source_url,
638	        cache_key=cache_key,
639	        status=result.status,
640	        details=result.details,
641	        new_canonical_url=result.new_canonical_url,
642	        fetched_status_code=result.fetched_status_code,
643	        checked_at=clock(),
644	        evicted_cache_key=evicted_key,
645	    )
646

664	        evicted_cache_key=row["evicted_cache_key"],
665	    )

1	"""V30 Phase-2 sweep wired into the JobRunner abstraction.
2	
3	Per FINAL_PLAN.md (Phase B M-9): launch the existing V30 Phase-2 sweep
4	as a subprocess, monitor run_log.txt for phase boundaries, emit
5	checkpoints at each boundary so pause/cancel work cooperatively.
6	
7	Design:
8	- Subprocess approach (NOT in-process refactor) because the sweep is
9	  ~5000 lines of asyncio orchestration and refactoring it phase-by-phase
10	  is a Phase C task.
11	- Phase boundaries derived from run_log.txt timestamps (the sweep
12	  already writes one line per phase). The runner tails the log and
13	  emits control.checkpoint() per phase line.
14	- Cancel: subprocess gets SIGTERM (or terminate() on Windows). Caller
15	  waits up to 30s for graceful shutdown then SIGKILL.
16	- Pause: NOT supported for V30 in Phase B. The sweep has no clean
17	  mid-sweep pause point. JobControl.Paused raises if pause is requested,
18	  which the worker converts to mark_paused — but the resume path will
19	  re-run from scratch (no incremental checkpoint state inside V30).
20	  Phase C M-13 progressive surfaces enable real pause via SSE streaming.
21	
22	Codex M-9 anticipates:
23	- Subprocess env passes OPENROUTER_API_KEY etc through to the child
24	- Python interpreter resolution is sys.executable (matches parent venv)
25	- Per-job artifact dir: out_root/<domain>/<slug>/ — resolves via the
26	  manifest_path field returned in checkpoint state
27	"""

166	    """Runtime configuration for the V30 runner."""

168	    repo_root: Path
169	    sweep_script: Path

171	    python_bin: str = sys.executable

177	class HonestSweepJobRunner(JobRunner):
178	    """Runs the V30 Phase-2 sweep as a subprocess, checkpoints per phase.
179	
180	    Job params (required): {"slug": "<canonical_slug>"}
181	    Job params (optional): {"domain": "<domain>"} — defaults derived
182	                                                    from slug map.
183	
184	    Returns: artifact_dir path on success.
185	    """
186	
187	    template_id = "v30_clinical"
188	
189	    def __init__(self, config: HonestSweepJobRunnerConfig) -> None:
190	        self._config = config
191	
192	    def run(self, job: Job, control: JobControl) -> str | None:
193	        slug = (job.params or {}).get("slug")
194	        if not slug:
195	            raise ValueError("v30_clinical: job.params.slug is required")
196	        cfg = self._config
197	        if not cfg.sweep_script.exists():
198	            raise FileNotFoundError(f"Sweep script missing: {cfg.sweep_script}")
199	
200	        # Codex M-9 review fix: per-job output root so concurrent or
201	        # sequential reruns of the same slug never overwrite each other.
202	        # Layout becomes out_root/<job_id>/<domain>/<slug>/.
203	        per_job_out_root = cfg.out_root / job.job_id
204	        per_job_out_root.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
205	
206	        # Subprocess env: inherit + add V30 env extras.
207	        env = dict(os.environ)
208	        if cfg.extra_env:
209	            env.update(cfg.extra_env)
210	
211	        cmd = [
212	            cfg.python_bin,
213	            str(cfg.sweep_script),
214	            "--only", slug,
215	            "--out-root", str(per_job_out_root),
216	        ]
217	
218	        # Initial checkpoint so the queue shows progress immediately.
219	        # Codex M-9 v2 review fix: guard this with the same Paused →
220	        # RuntimeError conversion as the loop checkpoints.
221	        try:
222	            control.checkpoint(
223	                progress_pct=1.0,
224	                message="Launching V30 sweep subprocess",
225	                state={"slug": slug, "phase": "launch"},
226	            )
227	        except JobControl.Cancelled:
228	            raise
229	        except JobControl.Paused:
230	            raise RuntimeError(
231	                "Pause is not supported for template_id='v30_clinical' "
232	                "in Phase B. Use cancel + re-enqueue instead."
233	            ) from None
234	
235	        # Codex M-13: emit a PREFLIGHT surface so the Inspector UI
236	        # can render scope/cost/time estimate immediately at t=0.
237	        # The actual estimates are derived from the slug + sweep
238	        # config; richer per-slug estimates land in Phase C.
239	        self._emit_surface(
240	            job, SurfaceKind.PREFLIGHT, {
241	                "slug": slug,
242	                "estimated_minutes": 145,  # FINAL_PLAN p90 target
243	                "cost_cap_usd": _read_env_float("PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN", 10.0),
244	                "phase": "launch",
245	            },
246	        )
247	
248	        logger.info("HonestSweepJobRunner: launching %s", " ".join(cmd))
249	        proc = subprocess.Popen(
250	            cmd,
251	            cwd=str(cfg.repo_root),
252	            env=env,
253	            stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
254	            stderr=subprocess.STDOUT,
255	            text=True,
256	        )
257	
258	        seen_phases: set[str] = set()
259	        last_phase = "launch"
260	        last_pct = 1.0
261	        # Tail the subprocess stdout in a thread so we can poll the
262	        # control surface in the main thread.
263	        log_lines: list[str] = []
264	        log_lock = threading.Lock()
265	
266	        def _drain() -> None:
267	            assert proc.stdout is not None
268	            for line in proc.stdout:
269	                with log_lock:
270	                    log_lines.append(line)
271	
272	        drain_thread = threading.Thread(target=_drain, daemon=True, name="v30-stdout-drain")
273	        drain_thread.start()
274	
275	        # Codex M-9 v3 review fix: subprocess termination MUST happen
276	        # in `finally`, not in individual `except` blocks. Earlier
277	        # iterations called `_terminate_subprocess` only inside
278	        # `except JobControl.Cancelled` / `except JobControl.Paused`.
279	        # That left a hole: when the inner per-phase or periodic
280	        # checkpoint converted Paused → RuntimeError, the RuntimeError
281	        # propagated past the outer Cancelled/Paused excepts (no match)
282	        # and the V30 child kept running in the background while the
283	        # job was marked failed. Centralizing termination in `finally`
284	        # closes that gap for ALL exit paths (Cancelled, Paused,
285	        # Paused→RuntimeError, any other RuntimeError, normal exit).
286	        # `_terminate_subprocess` is idempotent — it no-ops if the
287	        # process has already exited (proc.poll() is not None).
288	        try:
289	            while True:
290	                # Detect new phase boundaries from the latest log lines.
291	                with log_lock:
292	                    snapshot = list(log_lines)
293	                    log_lines.clear()
294	                for line in snapshot:
295	                    phase = self._classify_phase(line)
296	                    if phase and phase not in seen_phases:
297	                        seen_phases.add(phase)
298	                        last_phase = phase
299	                        last_pct = _PHASE_PCT[phase]
300	                        try:
301	                            control.checkpoint(
302	                                progress_pct=last_pct,
303	                                message=_PHASE_MSG[phase],
304	                                state={
305	                                    "slug": slug,
306	                                    "phase": phase,
307	                                    "log_line": line.strip()[:300],
308	                                },
309	                            )
310	                        except JobControl.Cancelled:
311	                            raise
312	                        except JobControl.Paused:
313	                            raise RuntimeError(
314	                                "Pause is not supported for template_id='v30_clinical' "
315	                                "in Phase B. Use cancel + re-enqueue instead."
316	                            ) from None
317	                        # Codex M-13: emit a progressive surface
318	                        # for the new phase. Phases that don't map
319	                        # to a user-visible milestone (PHASE_MAP
320	                        # entry is None) are skipped. The progress
321	                        # bar still moves via control.checkpoint
322	                        # above; surfaces are an additional view.
323	                        surface_kind = _PHASE_TO_SURFACE.get(phase)
324	                        if surface_kind is not None:
325	                            self._emit_surface(
326	                                job, surface_kind, {
327	                                    "slug": slug,
328	                                    "phase": phase,
329	                                    "progress_pct": last_pct,
330	                                    "message": _PHASE_MSG[phase],
331	                                    "log_line": line.strip()[:300],
332	                                },
333	                            )
334	
335	                # Periodic checkpoint even when no new phase fires
336	                # (so cancel/pause requests are detected within
337	                # poll_interval_s).
338	                try:
339	                    control.checkpoint(
340	                        progress_pct=last_pct,
341	                        message=_PHASE_MSG.get(last_phase, last_phase),
342	                        state={"slug": slug, "phase": last_phase},
343	                    )
344	                except JobControl.Cancelled:
345	                    raise
346	                except JobControl.Paused:
347	                    # Codex M-9 review fix: pause is not supported for
348	                    # V30 in Phase B. Convert to a hard failure so the
349	                    # user sees "Pause unsupported for v30_clinical"
350	                    # rather than ending up in a paused/resumable state
351	                    # that re-runs from scratch on resume.
352	                    raise RuntimeError(
353	                        "Pause is not supported for template_id='v30_clinical' "
354	                        "in Phase B. Use cancel + re-enqueue instead. "
355	                        "(Phase C M-13 progressive surfaces will enable "
356	                        "real pause via SSE streaming.)"
357	                    ) from None
358	
359	                # Subprocess done?
360	                rc = proc.poll()
361	                if rc is not None:
362	                    break
363	                time.sleep(cfg.poll_interval_s)
364	
365	        except JobControl.Paused:
366	            # Safety net: if Paused escapes through to the outer scope
367	            # (neither inner except converted it), convert here.
368	            raise RuntimeError(
369	                "Pause is not supported for template_id='v30_clinical' "
370	                "in Phase B. Use cancel + re-enqueue instead."
371	            ) from None
372	        finally:
373	            self._terminate_subprocess(proc, cfg.cancel_grace_s)
374	            drain_thread.join(timeout=2.0)
375	
376	        if proc.returncode != 0:
377	            tail = "".join(log_lines[-50:]) if log_lines else ""
378	            raise RuntimeError(
379	                f"V30 sweep failed (rc={proc.returncode}). Tail:\n{tail}"
380	            )
381	
382	        # Resolve the artifact dir. Per-job out_root means there's
383	        # exactly one valid <domain>/<slug>/ underneath; no mtime
384	        # tie-breaking needed.
385	        artifact_dir = self._resolve_artifact_dir(per_job_out_root, slug)
386	        return str(artifact_dir) if artifact_dir else None
387	
388	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
389	    # Helpers
390	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
391	
392	    @staticmethod
393	    def _emit_surface(
394	        job: Job, kind: SurfaceKind, payload: dict[str, Any]
395	    ) -> None:
396	        """Codex M-13: best-effort surface emission. Never let a
397	        bus error fail the audit run — surfaces are auxiliary."""
398	        try:
399	            get_surface_bus().emit(job.job_id, kind, payload)
400	        except Exception:  # pragma: no cover - defensive
401	            logger.exception("surface emit failed for job %s", job.job_id)
402	
403	    @staticmethod
404	    def _classify_phase(line: str) -> str | None:
405	        lc = line.lower()
406	        for phase_key, pattern in _PHASE_PATTERNS:
407	            if pattern.lower() in lc:
408	                return phase_key
409	        return None
410	
411	    @staticmethod
412	    def _terminate_subprocess(proc: subprocess.Popen, grace_s: float) -> None:
413	        if proc.poll() is not None:
414	            return
415	        try:
416	            proc.terminate()  # SIGTERM on POSIX, TerminateProcess on Windows
417	        except Exception:
418	            pass
419	        try:
420	            proc.wait(timeout=grace_s)
421	        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
422	            try:
423	                proc.kill()
424	                proc.wait(timeout=5.0)
425	            except Exception:
426	                pass
427	
428	    @staticmethod
429	    def _resolve_artifact_dir(out_root: Path, slug: str) -> Path | None:
430	        """Find the artifact directory for the given slug.
431	
432	        Layout: out_root/<domain>/<slug>/. We glob for the slug match
433	        and return the most-recently-modified candidate.
434	        """
435	        if not out_root.is_dir():
436	            return None
437	        candidates: list[Path] = []
438	        for domain_dir in out_root.iterdir():
439	            if not domain_dir.is_dir():
440	                continue
441	            slug_dir = domain_dir / slug
442	            if slug_dir.is_dir() and (slug_dir / "manifest.json").exists():
443	                candidates.append(slug_dir)
444	        if not candidates:
445	            return None
446	        candidates.sort(key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime, reverse=True)
447	        return candidates[0]
448	
449	
450	def make_default_honest_sweep_job_runner(repo_root: Path | None = None) -> HonestSweepJobRunner:
451	    """Convenience factory pointing at the canonical sweep script."""
452	    if repo_root is None:
453	        # repo_root = src/polaris_graph/audit_ir/honest_sweep_job_runner.py.parents[3]
454	        repo_root = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3]
455	    config = HonestSweepJobRunnerConfig(
456	        repo_root=repo_root,
457	        sweep_script=repo_root / "scripts" / "run_full_scale_v30_phase2.py",
458	        out_root=repo_root / "outputs" / "polaris_v30_jobs",
459	    )
460	    return HonestSweepJobRunner(config)

1	"""Durable SQLite-backed job queue for Phase B.
2	
3	Per FINAL_PLAN.md (jointly Codex+Claude GREEN): Phase B requires queue-
4	backed concurrency + pause/cancel/resume mid-run. This module is the
5	foundation — Phase A's `PipelineRunner` single-concurrency lock is
6	replaced with this queue, and the Inspector router exposes
7	job lifecycle endpoints.
8	
9	Status state machine:
10	    pending → running → completed
11	                     → cancelled
12	                     → failed
13	                     → paused → running → completed / cancelled / failed
14	
15	Design constraints:
16	- SQLite stdlib only (no Redis dep yet — Phase C may upgrade)
17	- All ops thread-safe via per-call connection + WAL mode
18	- Pause/cancel are REQUEST flags that workers check at checkpoint
19	  boundaries (workers cooperatively yield; no signal/kill semantics)
20	- Checkpoints are JSON blobs — workers serialize their internal state
21	  so restart-after-crash + resume-after-pause use the same code path
22	
23	Codex M-8 design choices (anticipating review):
24	- run_id (UUID4) is the canonical identifier; slug-style identifiers
25	  belong in the registry, not the queue
26	- claim_pending() is the worker pull primitive; uses SQLite's
27	  atomic UPDATE-WHERE-status='pending' to prevent two workers
28	  picking the same job
29	- All timestamps are float Unix epoch seconds (UTC)
30	- All status transitions are explicit; the queue refuses invalid
31	  ones to fail loud rather than silently corrupt state
32	"""
33	
34	from __future__ import annotations
35	
36	import json
37	import sqlite3
38	import threading
39	import time
40	import uuid
41	from contextlib import contextmanager
42	from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass, field
43	from pathlib import Path
44	from typing import Any, Iterator, Mapping
45	
46	# All valid job statuses, in canonical order.
47	JOB_STATUSES = (
48	    "pending",
49	    "running",
50	    "paused",
51	    "completed",
52	    "cancelled",
53	    "failed",
54	)
55	TERMINAL_STATUSES = frozenset({"completed", "cancelled", "failed"})
56	
57	# Canonical state-transition graph. Other transitions raise.
58	#
59	# Codex M-8 review fix: paused -> running is NOT a real edge under the
60	# current "no worker heartbeat" design. After JobControl.Paused exits
61	# runner.run(), the worker thread returns and no live executor is
62	# attached to the paused row. Resume therefore goes paused -> pending
63	# so a fresh worker can claim it via claim_pending() and re-enter
64	# runner.run(job, control) with job.checkpoint providing the resume
65	# state. This also makes paused -> cancelled direct (paused jobs are
66	# quiescent, so no need to ask a non-existent worker to honor a flag).
67	ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS: dict[str, frozenset[str]] = {
68	    "pending": frozenset({"running", "cancelled"}),
69	    "running": frozenset({"paused", "completed", "cancelled", "failed"}),
70	    "paused": frozenset({"pending", "cancelled"}),
71	    "completed": frozenset(),  # terminal
72	    "cancelled": frozenset(),
73	    "failed": frozenset(),
74	}

89	    to `org_default` / None, making the M-15b authz dependency

93	    job_id: str
94	    template_id: str
95	    params: Mapping[str, Any]
96	    status: str
97	    created_at: float
98	    started_at: float | None
99	    paused_at: float | None

138	def _row_to_job(row: sqlite3.Row) -> Job:

140	    # migration still work even if a row materialized before the
141	    # ALTER picked up.
142	    org_id = "org_default"

173	class JobQueue:
174	    """Durable SQLite-backed queue. Thread-safe via per-call connections + WAL."""
175	
176	    def __init__(self, db_path: Path | str) -> None:
177	        self._db_path = Path(db_path)
178	        self._db_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
179	        self._init_lock = threading.Lock()
180	        self._initialized = False
181	        self._ensure_schema()
182	
183	    def _ensure_schema(self) -> None:
184	        with self._init_lock:
185	            if self._initialized:
186	                return
187	            with self._connect() as conn:
188	                conn.executescript("PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL;")
189	                conn.executescript(_SCHEMA_SQL)
190	                # Codex M-15b review fix: migration for pre-M-15b
191	                # databases. CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS is a no-op
192	                # when the table exists, so old DBs need ALTER.
193	                cols = [
194	                    r[1] for r in conn.execute(
195	                        "PRAGMA table_info(jobs)"
196	                    ).fetchall()
197	                ]
198	                if "org_id" not in cols:
199	                    conn.execute(
200	                        "ALTER TABLE jobs ADD COLUMN org_id TEXT NOT NULL "
201	                        "DEFAULT 'org_default'"
202	                    )
203	                if "workspace_id" not in cols:
204	                    conn.execute(
205	                        "ALTER TABLE jobs ADD COLUMN workspace_id TEXT"
206	                    )
207	                conn.execute(

212	    @contextmanager
213	    def _connect(self) -> Iterator[sqlite3.Connection]:
214	        conn = sqlite3.connect(self._db_path, timeout=10.0, isolation_level=None)
215	        conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
216	        try:
217	            yield conn

225	    def enqueue(
226	        self,
227	        template_id: str,
228	        params: Mapping[str, Any],
229	        org_id: str = "org_default",
230	        workspace_id: str | None = None,
231	    ) -> Job:
232	        """Create a new pending job. Returns the persisted Job.
233	
234	        Codex M-15b review fix: `org_id` and `workspace_id` are
235	        now parameters. Pre-M-15b callers without auth context
236	        default to `org_default` / None — the M-15b retrofit
237	        treats those as the notional system org so cross-org
238	        gates still apply consistently.
239	        """
240	        if not template_id:
241	            raise JobQueueError("enqueue: template_id required")
242	        if not isinstance(params, Mapping):
243	            raise JobQueueError("enqueue: params must be a Mapping")
244	        if not org_id or not org_id.strip():
245	            raise JobQueueError("enqueue: org_id must be non-empty")
246	        job_id = str(uuid.uuid4())

249	            conn.execute(
250	                "INSERT INTO jobs (job_id, template_id, params_json, "
251	                "status, created_at, org_id, workspace_id) "
252	                "VALUES (?, ?, ?, 'pending', ?, ?, ?)",
253	                (job_id, template_id, json.dumps(dict(params)), now,

308	    def claim_pending(self) -> Job | None:
309	        """Atomically claim a pending job for a worker. Returns None if none.
310	
311	        Implementation: UPDATE WHERE status='pending' is atomic in SQLite;
312	        we use last_insert_rowid trick is not portable, so we use:
313	        - SELECT job_id WHERE status='pending' ORDER BY created_at LIMIT 1
314	        - UPDATE WHERE job_id=? AND status='pending' SET status='running'
315	        - check changes() == 1
316	        """
317	        with self._connect() as conn:
318	            row = conn.execute(
319	                "SELECT job_id FROM jobs WHERE status='pending' "
320	                "ORDER BY created_at ASC LIMIT 1"
321	            ).fetchone()
322	            if row is None:
323	                return None
324	            now = time.time()
325	            cursor = conn.execute(
326	                "UPDATE jobs SET status='running', started_at=? "
327	                "WHERE job_id=? AND status='pending'",
328	                (now, row["job_id"]),
329	            )
330	            if cursor.rowcount != 1:
331	                # Another worker grabbed it. Caller should retry.
332	                return None
333	        return self._must_get(row["job_id"])

335	    def request_pause(self, job_id: str) -> Job:
336	        """Set the pause_requested flag. Worker will pause at next checkpoint."""
337	        with self._connect() as conn:
338	            cursor = conn.execute(
339	                "UPDATE jobs SET pause_requested=1 "
340	                "WHERE job_id=? AND status='running'",
341	                (job_id,),
342	            )
343	            if cursor.rowcount == 0:
344	                # Either job doesn't exist or isn't running; surface clearly.
345	                job = self.get(job_id)
346	                if job is None:
347	                    raise JobQueueError(f"request_pause: unknown job {job_id}")
348	                raise JobQueueError(
349	                    f"request_pause: job {job_id} status={job.status} cannot be paused"
350	                )
351	        return self._must_get(job_id)

353	    def request_cancel(self, job_id: str) -> Job:
354	        """Cancel a job.
355	
356	        Codex M-8 review fix: pending and paused jobs are quiescent (no
357	        live worker), so we transition them directly to 'cancelled'.
358	        Running jobs get the cancel_requested flag and the worker
359	        cooperatively yields at the next checkpoint.
360	        """
361	        with self._connect() as conn:
362	            now = time.time()
363	            # Pending or paused -> directly mark cancelled (no live worker).
364	            cursor = conn.execute(
365	                "UPDATE jobs SET status='cancelled', cancel_requested=1, completed_at=? "
366	                "WHERE job_id=? AND status IN ('pending', 'paused')",
367	                (now, job_id),
368	            )
369	            if cursor.rowcount == 1:
370	                return self._must_get(job_id)
371	            # Running -> set the flag; worker yields at next checkpoint.
372	            cursor = conn.execute(
373	                "UPDATE jobs SET cancel_requested=1 "
374	                "WHERE job_id=? AND status='running'",
375	                (job_id,),
376	            )
377	            if cursor.rowcount == 0:
378	                job = self.get(job_id)
379	                if job is None:
380	                    raise JobQueueError(f"request_cancel: unknown job {job_id}")
381	                raise JobQueueError(
382	                    f"request_cancel: job {job_id} status={job.status} cannot be cancelled"
383	                )
384	        return self._must_get(job_id)

386	    def resume_paused(self, job_id: str) -> Job:
387	        """Transition paused -> pending so a fresh worker can claim it.
388	
389	        Codex M-8 review fix: under the cooperative-yield design, the
390	        original worker exited runner.run() when Paused fired. To resume,
391	        we put the job back in the pending queue with the checkpoint
392	        intact; claim_pending() will pick it up and the runner re-enters
393	        from job.checkpoint.
394	        """
395	        with self._connect() as conn:
396	            cursor = conn.execute(
397	                "UPDATE jobs SET status='pending', pause_requested=0, paused_at=NULL "
398	                "WHERE job_id=? AND status='paused'",
399	                (job_id,),
400	            )
401	            if cursor.rowcount == 0:
402	                job = self.get(job_id)
403	                if job is None:
404	                    raise JobQueueError(f"resume_paused: unknown job {job_id}")
405	                raise JobQueueError(
406	                    f"resume_paused: job {job_id} status={job.status} is not paused"
407	                )

474	    def record_progress(
475	        self,
476	        job_id: str,

479	        checkpoint: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None,

487	        with self._connect() as conn:

490	                "WHERE job_id=? AND status='running'",

495	                    job_id,

497	            )

511	    def _must_get(self, job_id: str) -> Job:
512	        job = self.get(job_id)
513	        if job is None:
514	            raise JobQueueError(f"job {job_id} disappeared after write")
515	        return job

517	    def _transition(
518	        self,
519	        job_id: str,
520	        from_status: str,
521	        to_status: str,
522	        **fields: Any,
523	    ) -> None:
524	        if to_status not in ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS.get(from_status, frozenset()):
525	            raise JobQueueError(
526	                f"_transition: illegal {from_status} -> {to_status}"
527	            )
528	        cols = ["status=?"] + [f"{k}=?" for k in fields.keys()]
529	        vals = [to_status] + list(fields.values()) + [job_id, from_status]
530	        with self._connect() as conn:
531	            cursor = conn.execute(
532	                f"UPDATE jobs SET {', '.join(cols)} "
533	                f"WHERE job_id=? AND status=?",
534	                vals,
535	            )
536	            if cursor.rowcount == 0:
537	                job = self.get(job_id)
538	                if job is None:
539	                    raise JobQueueError(f"_transition: unknown job {job_id}")
540	                raise JobQueueError(
541	                    f"_transition: job {job_id} status={job.status}, "
542	                    f"expected {from_status}"
543	                )

552	            conn.execute("DELETE FROM jobs")

1	"""M-D11 (Phase D): Model + version pinning.
2	
3	Per FINAL_PLAN M-D11: every audit bundle records exact model
4	versions, prompt versions, retrieval-source versions, plus
5	inductor type + version (Phase D specific) AND environment
6	toggles that affect routing/prompt selection. Re-run-from-pin
7	capability lets a future operator reproduce a historical run.
8	
9	This module ships **phase 1 — pin capture and serialization**.
10	Phase 2 (replay: load pin → configure pipeline to match → run)
11	is a separate module that depends on this one being stable.
12	
13	## Schema version
14	
15	`PIN_SCHEMA_VERSION = "v4"`. Every pin carries this version
16	explicitly. `pin_from_dict` rejects mismatched versions loudly
17	so future schema changes don't silently misload historical pins.
18	
19	## Pin shape (v4)
20	
21	ModelPin captures:
22	  - run_id: the run this pin was captured from
23	  - captured_at: unix timestamp
24	  - pin_schema_version: "v4" (explicit forward-compat marker)
25	  - llm_models: {role: model_id} — e.g. {"generator": "deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro",
26	    "evaluator": "google/gemma-4-31b-it", "judge": "...", "inductor": "..."}.
27	    The current honest_pipeline + audit_ir loader carry distinct
28	    generator/evaluator/judge models; phase 2 needs all of them
29	    to rehydrate.
30	  - llm_providers: {role: provider} — auto-filled with "openrouter"
31	    for each role unless overridden.
32	  - prompt_version_hashes: {role: SHA-256 hex} — empty for roles
33	    without a captured system prompt (allowed, since some roles
34	    may use the model's default prompt).
35	  - retrieval_source_versions: dict of {source: version}
36	    (e.g. {"crossref": "v1", "pubmed": "2024-12", "unpaywall": "v2"})
37	  - inductor_type: which auto-induction inductor was active
38	    (e.g. "KeywordInductor v5" or "LLMAugmentedInductor")
39	  - inductor_version_hash: hash of the inductor's keyword profile
40	    or LLM classifier prompt — captures structural changes that
41	    would alter routing behavior
42	  - validation_set_hash: hash of the M-D1 validation set file
43	    if the run involved induction
44	  - env_snapshot: {var_name: value} captured environment toggles
45	    that affect routing, prompt selection, verification gates, AND
46	    LLM call profile (token budgets).
47	
48	    **Unset vs. empty distinction**: an unset env var is captured
49	    as `None`; a var explicitly set to "" is captured as `""`.
50	    Many call sites read numeric vars via `int(os.getenv("X",
51	    "default"))`, so phase-2 replay must DELETE the env var (not
52	    set it to "") when the captured value is `None`. Conflating
53	    the two would either (a) crash `int("")` at the call site, or
54	    (b) bypass the runtime default and silently change behavior.
55	    See `DEFAULT_REPLAY_ENV_VARS` for the recommended capture set
56	    (verified against actual `os.getenv` call sites).
57	  - notes: free-form operator context (excluded from replay
58	    equivalence)
59	
60	The pin is JSON-serializable and round-trips cleanly. Re-run-
61	from-pin (M-D11 phase 2) loads this and configures the pipeline.
62	
63	## Why pin separately from M-16 audit bundle
64	
65	M-16's audit bundle captures evidence + claims + provenance —
66	the WHAT of an audit. M-D11 captures the HOW: the
67	configuration of generators, retrievers, inductors, and the
68	runtime environment that influenced their behavior. This split
69	matters for Phase D governance (M-D9 regression lab) which
70	diffs pin-to-pin to spot configuration drift independent of
71	content drift.
72	"""
73
