178	@dataclass(frozen=True)
179	class ModelPin:
180	    """One audit run's model + version + configuration pin.
181	
182	    Frozen because pin records are immutable once written —
183	    replaying a run requires the pin's exact bit-pattern at
184	    capture time. Mutating in place would lose reproducibility.
185	    """
186	
187	    run_id: str
188	    captured_at: float
189	    pin_schema_version: str = PIN_SCHEMA_VERSION
190	    llm_models: dict[str, str] = field(default_factory=dict)
191	    llm_providers: dict[str, str] = field(default_factory=dict)
192	    prompt_version_hashes: dict[str, str] = field(default_factory=dict)
193	    retrieval_source_versions: dict[str, str] = field(default_factory=dict)
194	    inductor_type: str | None = None
195	    inductor_version_hash: str | None = None
196	    validation_set_hash: str | None = None
197	    # str -> str | None. None = "var was unset at capture time".
198	    # "" = "var was set to empty string". Phase-2 replay must
199	    # respect the distinction (delete vs. set) — see module
200	    # docstring.
201	    env_snapshot: dict[str, str | None] = field(default_factory=dict)
202	    # Free-form notes for operator context — not used by replay,
203	    # only for human readability.
204	    notes: str = ""
205	
206	
207	class ModelPinError(ValueError):
208	    """Raised on malformed pin schema / serialization issues."""
209	
210	
211	def _hash_text(text: str) -> str:
212	    """SHA-256 hex digest of UTF-8-encoded text. Used for prompt
213	    hashes + inductor version hashes."""
214	    return hashlib.sha256(text.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
215	
216	
217	def hash_file(path: Path | str) -> str:
218	    """SHA-256 of a file. Used for validation_set_hash and any
219	    other content-addressable artifact pin."""
220	    p = Path(path)
221	    if not p.exists():
222	        raise ModelPinError(f"file does not exist: {p}")
223	    h = hashlib.sha256()
224	    with p.open("rb") as fp:
225	        for chunk in iter(lambda: fp.read(65536), b""):
226	            h.update(chunk)
227	    return h.hexdigest()
228	
229	
230	def hash_inductor_profile(profile_text: str) -> str:
231	    """Hash an inductor's keyword profile / system prompt /
232	    config text. Any structural change that would alter routing
233	    decisions changes the hash."""
234	    return _hash_text(profile_text)
235	
236	
237	def capture_env_snapshot(
238	    names: Iterable[str] = DEFAULT_REPLAY_ENV_VARS,
239	) -> dict[str, str | None]:
240	    """Capture current values of named environment variables.
241	
242	    Returns `None` for unset variables and the string value for
243	    set variables (including empty string). The distinction
244	    matters: many call sites read numeric vars with a default
245	    fallback (`int(os.getenv("X", "8192"))`) — re-setting to ""
246	    on replay would skip the default and crash the cast.
247	    """
248	    snapshot: dict[str, str | None] = {}
249	    for name in names:
250	        if not isinstance(name, str) or not name.strip():
251	            raise ModelPinError(
252	                "env var names must be non-empty strings"
253	            )
254	        clean = name.strip()
255	        # `None` if unset; the actual value (possibly "") if set.
256	        snapshot[clean] = os.environ.get(clean)
257	    return snapshot
258	
259	
260	def _validate_role_dict(
261	    field_name: str,
262	    data: Any,
263	    *,
264	    require_non_empty: bool = True,
265	) -> dict[str, str]:
266	    """Validate a role-keyed dict: keys+values are non-empty
267	    strings. Returns a normalized (stripped-key/value) dict."""
268	    if not isinstance(data, dict):
269	        raise ModelPinError(
270	            f"{field_name} must be a dict, got {type(data).__name__}"
271	        )
272	    if require_non_empty and not data:
273	        raise ModelPinError(f"{field_name} must be non-empty")
274	    out: dict[str, str] = {}
275	    for role, value in data.items():
276	        if not isinstance(role, str) or not role.strip():
277	            raise ModelPinError(
278	                f"{field_name} role keys must be non-empty strings"
279	            )
280	        if not isinstance(value, str) or not value.strip():
281	            raise ModelPinError(
282	                f"{field_name}[{role!r}] must be non-empty string"
283	            )
284	        out[role.strip()] = value.strip()
285	    return out
286	
287	
288	def _validate_retrieval_source_versions(data: Any) -> dict[str, str]:
289	    """Validate retrieval_source_versions: dict of str→str. Empty
290	    dict is allowed (no retrieval sources)."""
291	    if not isinstance(data, dict):
292	        raise ModelPinError(
293	            f"retrieval_source_versions must be a dict, "
294	            f"got {type(data).__name__}"
295	        )
296	    out: dict[str, str] = {}
297	    for k, v in data.items():
298	        if not isinstance(k, str) or not k.strip():
299	            raise ModelPinError(
300	                "retrieval_source_versions keys must be non-empty strings"
301	            )
302	        if not isinstance(v, str):
303	            raise ModelPinError(
304	                f"retrieval_source_versions[{k!r}] must be string, "
305	                f"got {type(v).__name__}"
306	            )
307	        out[k] = v
308	    return out
309	
310	
311	def _validate_env_snapshot(data: Any) -> dict[str, str | None]:
312	    """Validate env_snapshot shape: str → (str | None).
313	
314	    `None` is preserved (means "var was unset at capture time").
315	    Empty string is preserved (means "var was set to "")."""
316	    if not isinstance(data, dict):
317	        raise ModelPinError(
318	            f"env_snapshot must be a dict, got {type(data).__name__}"
319	        )
320	    out: dict[str, str | None] = {}
321	    for k, v in data.items():
322	        if not isinstance(k, str) or not k.strip():
323	            raise ModelPinError(
324	                "env_snapshot keys must be non-empty strings"
325	            )
326	        if v is None:
327	            out[k.strip()] = None
328	        elif isinstance(v, str):
329	            out[k.strip()] = v
330	        else:
331	            raise ModelPinError(
332	                f"env_snapshot[{k!r}] must be string or None, "
333	                f"got {type(v).__name__}"
334	            )
335	    return out
336	
337	
338	def capture_pin(
339	    *,
340	    run_id: str,
341	    llm_models: dict[str, str],
342	    llm_providers: dict[str, str] | None = None,
343	    role_prompts: dict[str, str] | None = None,
344	    retrieval_source_versions: dict[str, str] | None = None,
345	    inductor_type: str | None = None,
346	    inductor_profile_text: str | None = None,
347	    validation_set_path: Path | str | None = None,
348	    env_snapshot: dict[str, str | None] | None = None,
349	    capture_env_var_names: Iterable[str] | None = None,
350	    notes: str = "",
351	    captured_at: float | None = None,
352	) -> ModelPin:
353	    """Capture a ModelPin from the current runtime configuration.
354	
355	    `llm_models` is a {role: model_id} dict — at minimum one role
356	    (typically "generator") must be set. The honest_pipeline
357	    today separates generator/evaluator/judge/inductor; pass each
358	    one's model id under the corresponding role key.
359	
360	    Hashes are computed lazily — pass `role_prompts` text and let
361	    this function hash it, rather than passing pre-computed
362	    hashes. That way the pin captures the actual prompt text
363	    rather than trusting the caller's hash.
364	
365	    `env_snapshot` and `capture_env_var_names` are mutually
366	    exclusive: pass `env_snapshot` to provide a pre-captured
367	    snapshot, or `capture_env_var_names` to capture now from
368	    `os.environ`. If neither is passed, env_snapshot is empty.
369	    """
370	    if not run_id.strip():
371	        raise ModelPinError("run_id must be non-empty")
372	
373	    models = _validate_role_dict("llm_models", llm_models)
374	
375	    providers_in = (
376	        _validate_role_dict(
377	            "llm_providers", llm_providers, require_non_empty=False
378	        )
379	        if llm_providers is not None
380	        else {}
381	    )
382	    unknown_provider_roles = set(providers_in) - set(models)
383	    if unknown_provider_roles:
384	        raise ModelPinError(
385	            f"llm_providers has unknown roles "
386	            f"(not in llm_models): {sorted(unknown_provider_roles)}"
387	        )
388	    providers = dict(providers_in)
389	    for role in models:
390	        providers.setdefault(role, "openrouter")
391	
392	    prompt_hashes: dict[str, str] = {}
393	    if role_prompts is not None:
394	        prompts_validated = _validate_role_dict(
395	            "role_prompts", role_prompts, require_non_empty=False
396	        )
397	        unknown_prompt_roles = set(prompts_validated) - set(models)
398	        if unknown_prompt_roles:
399	            raise ModelPinError(
400	                f"role_prompts has unknown roles "
401	                f"(not in llm_models): {sorted(unknown_prompt_roles)}"
402	            )
403	        for role, text in prompts_validated.items():
404	            prompt_hashes[role] = _hash_text(text)
405	
406	    inductor_hash = (
407	        hash_inductor_profile(inductor_profile_text)
408	        if inductor_profile_text is not None
409	        else None
410	    )
411	    vs_hash = (
412	        hash_file(validation_set_path)
413	        if validation_set_path is not None
414	        else None
415	    )
416	
417	    if env_snapshot is not None and capture_env_var_names is not None:
418	        raise ModelPinError(
419	            "pass env_snapshot or capture_env_var_names, not both"
420	        )
421	    if env_snapshot is not None:
422	        env_final = _validate_env_snapshot(env_snapshot)
423	    elif capture_env_var_names is not None:
424	        env_final = capture_env_snapshot(capture_env_var_names)
425	    else:
426	        env_final = {}
427	
428	    retrieval_validated = _validate_retrieval_source_versions(
429	        retrieval_source_versions or {}
430	    )
431	
432	    return ModelPin(
433	        run_id=run_id.strip(),
434	        captured_at=captured_at if captured_at is not None else time.time(),
435	        pin_schema_version=PIN_SCHEMA_VERSION,
436	        llm_models=models,
437	        llm_providers=providers,
438	        prompt_version_hashes=prompt_hashes,
439	        retrieval_source_versions=retrieval_validated,
440	        inductor_type=inductor_type,
441	        inductor_version_hash=inductor_hash,
442	        validation_set_hash=vs_hash,
443	        env_snapshot=env_final,
444	        notes=notes,
445	    )
446	
447	
448	def pin_to_dict(pin: ModelPin) -> dict[str, Any]:
449	    """JSON-safe dict representation."""
450	    return asdict(pin)
451	
452	
453	def pin_to_json(pin: ModelPin, *, indent: int = 2) -> str:
454	    """Serialize a pin to JSON with stable key ordering."""
455	    return json.dumps(pin_to_dict(pin), indent=indent, sort_keys=True)
456	
457	
458	def pin_from_dict(data: dict[str, Any]) -> ModelPin:
459	    """Reconstruct a ModelPin from its dict form, re-applying
460	    every invariant that capture_pin enforces.
461	
462	    This is symmetric with capture_pin: a malformed dict must NOT
463	    produce a ModelPin that compares/serializes as if it were
464	    valid. Empty role dicts, missing required keys, and schema-
465	    version mismatches all raise ModelPinError.
466	    """
467	    if not isinstance(data, dict):
468	        raise ModelPinError(
469	            f"pin data must be a dict; got {type(data).__name__}"
470	        )
471	
472	    schema_version = data.get("pin_schema_version")
473	    if schema_version != PIN_SCHEMA_VERSION:
474	        raise ModelPinError(
475	            f"pin_schema_version mismatch: got {schema_version!r}, "
476	            f"expected {PIN_SCHEMA_VERSION!r}"
477	        )
478	
479	    required = ("run_id", "captured_at", "llm_models")
480	    for key in required:
481	        if key not in data:
482	            raise ModelPinError(f"pin data missing required key: {key!r}")
483	
484	    try:
485	        run_id_raw = data["run_id"]
486	        if not isinstance(run_id_raw, str) or not run_id_raw.strip():
487	            raise ModelPinError("run_id must be non-empty string")
488	        run_id = run_id_raw.strip()
489	
490	        captured_at = float(data["captured_at"])
491	
492	        llm_models = _validate_role_dict("llm_models", data["llm_models"])
493	
494	        providers_raw = data.get("llm_providers", {}) or {}
495	        llm_providers = _validate_role_dict(
496	            "llm_providers", providers_raw, require_non_empty=False
497	        )
498	        unknown_p = set(llm_providers) - set(llm_models)
499	        if unknown_p:
500	            raise ModelPinError(
501	                f"llm_providers has unknown roles: {sorted(unknown_p)}"
502	            )
503	        missing_p = set(llm_models) - set(llm_providers)
504	        if missing_p:
505	            raise ModelPinError(
506	                f"llm_providers missing roles "
507	                f"(every model role needs a provider): {sorted(missing_p)}"
508	            )
509	
510	        prompts_raw = data.get("prompt_version_hashes", {}) or {}
511	        prompt_version_hashes = _validate_role_dict(
512	            "prompt_version_hashes", prompts_raw, require_non_empty=False
513	        )
514	        unknown_h = set(prompt_version_hashes) - set(llm_models)
515	        if unknown_h:
516	            raise ModelPinError(
517	                f"prompt_version_hashes has unknown roles: {sorted(unknown_h)}"
518	            )
519	
520	        retrieval = _validate_retrieval_source_versions(
521	            data.get("retrieval_source_versions") or {}
522	        )
523	
524	        env_raw = data.get("env_snapshot", {}) or {}
525	        env_snapshot = _validate_env_snapshot(env_raw)
526	
527	        return ModelPin(
528	            run_id=run_id,
529	            captured_at=captured_at,
530	            pin_schema_version=str(schema_version),
531	            llm_models=llm_models,
532	            llm_providers=llm_providers,
533	            prompt_version_hashes=prompt_version_hashes,
534	            retrieval_source_versions=retrieval,
535	            inductor_type=(
536	                str(data["inductor_type"])
537	                if data.get("inductor_type") is not None
538	                else None
539	            ),
540	            inductor_version_hash=(
541	                str(data["inductor_version_hash"])
542	                if data.get("inductor_version_hash") is not None
543	                else None
544	            ),
545	            validation_set_hash=(
546	                str(data["validation_set_hash"])
547	                if data.get("validation_set_hash") is not None
548	                else None
549	            ),
550	            env_snapshot=env_snapshot,
551	            notes=str(data.get("notes", "")),
552	        )
553	    except ModelPinError:
554	        raise
555	    except (TypeError, ValueError) as exc:
556	        raise ModelPinError(f"pin data schema violation: {exc}") from exc
557	
558	
559	def pin_from_json(text: str) -> ModelPin:
560	    """Parse + validate JSON pin."""
561	    try:
562	        data = json.loads(text)
563	    except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
564	        raise ModelPinError(f"pin JSON decode failed: {exc}") from exc
565	    return pin_from_dict(data)
566	
567	
568	def pins_equivalent_for_replay(a: ModelPin, b: ModelPin) -> bool:
569	    """Two pins are replay-equivalent if all configuration-
570	    affecting fields match. `run_id`, `captured_at`, and `notes`
571	    are excluded — they're metadata, not configuration. This is
572	    the function M-D11 phase 2 (replay) uses to decide whether a
573	    new run reproduces a historical pin's exact configuration.
574	
575	    Note: `pin_schema_version` IS compared — different schema
576	    versions are not replay-equivalent (they encode different
577	    sets of fields).
578	    """
579	    return (
580	        a.pin_schema_version == b.pin_schema_version
581	        and a.llm_models == b.llm_models
582	        and a.llm_providers == b.llm_providers
583	        and a.prompt_version_hashes == b.prompt_version_hashes
584	        and a.retrieval_source_versions == b.retrieval_source_versions
585	        and a.inductor_type == b.inductor_type
586	        and a.inductor_version_hash == b.inductor_version_hash
587	        and a.validation_set_hash == b.validation_set_hash
588	        and a.env_snapshot == b.env_snapshot
589	    )
590

1	"""Load a V30 Phase-2 run artifact directory into a canonical AuditIR object.
2	
3	The AuditIR is the single source of truth for the Evidence Inspector and
4	all derivative renderers. It joins:
5	- manifest.json (run metadata, frame_coverage_report, corpus tier fractions,
6	  evaluator gate, v30 warnings, retrieval stats)
7	- report.md (the rendered markdown report with [N] inline citations)
8	- bibliography.json (the [N] -> evidence_id -> source mapping)
9	- contradictions.json (the 14 tier-labeled disagreement clusters with severity)
10	- verification_details.json (per-section sentences + evidence-span tokens —
11	  this is the foundation for Evidence Inspector View 1 click-to-inspect)
12	
13	into a single immutable object with lookup methods.
14	
15	Per FINAL_PLAN.md (jointly Claude+Codex agreed):
16	- The audit graph IR is canonical (NOT the report)
17	- Evidence Inspector is the primary renderer
18	- All other outputs are derivative projections with back-links to claim IDs
19	
20	Fail-loud semantics: missing or malformed required schema blocks raise
21	loudly. There is no silent zero-fill on canonical structures. This is the
22	audit-grade discipline applied to the IR itself.
23	"""
24	
25	from __future__ import annotations
26	
27	import json
28	from dataclasses import dataclass
29	from pathlib import Path
30	from types import MappingProxyType
31	from typing import Any, Mapping
32	
33	# Bumped on schema-breaking changes. Renderers should check this and refuse
34	# to render IR they don't know how to project.
35	IR_SCHEMA_VERSION = "1.0.0"
36	
37	
38	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
39	# Bibliography
40	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
41	
42	
43	@dataclass(frozen=True)
44	class BibliographyEntry:
45	    """A single source in the bibliography — the [N] -> source mapping."""
46	
47	    num: int
48	    evidence_id: str
49	    statement: str
50	    tier: str
51	    url: str
52	
53	
54	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
55	# Verified report (verification_details.json)
56	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
57	
58	
59	@dataclass(frozen=True)
60	class EvidenceSpanToken:
61	    """A `[#ev:<evidence_id>:<start>-<end>]` token bound to a sentence."""
62	
63	    evidence_id: str
64	    start: int
65	    end: int
66	
67	
68	@dataclass(frozen=True)
69	class ReportSentence:
70	    """One sentence in the verified report — kept or dropped.
71	
72	    `claim_id` is stable for the same artifact_dir: `<section>:<status>:<idx>`.
73	    Renderers (Evidence Inspector view 1) overlay these IDs onto the rendered
74	    `report.md` prose and use them as the click-to-inspect handle.
75	    """
76	
77	    claim_id: str
78	    section: str
79	    text: str
80	    tokens: tuple[EvidenceSpanToken, ...]
81	    is_verified: bool
82	    failure_reasons: tuple[str, ...]
83	
84	
85	@dataclass(frozen=True)
86	class ReportSection:
87	    """One section in the verified report — title plus all its sentences."""
88	
89	    title: str
90	    kept_count: int
91	    dropped_count: int
92	    total_in: int
93	    dropped_due_to_failure: int
94	    sentences: tuple[ReportSentence, ...]
95	
96	
97	@dataclass(frozen=True)
98	class VerifiedReport:
99	    """The full per-sentence verified report.
100	
101	    `drop_reason_counts` is a read-only mapping from reason -> count.
102	    """
103	
104	    sections: tuple[ReportSection, ...]
105	    sentences_verified: int
106	    sentences_dropped: int
107	    drop_reason_counts: Mapping[str, int]
108	
109	
110	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
111	# Contradictions
112	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
113	
114	
115	@dataclass(frozen=True)
116	class ContradictionClaim:
117	    """One claim inside a contradiction cluster."""
118	
119	    evidence_id: str
120	    subject: str
121	    predicate: str
122	    arm: str
123	    dose: str
124	    value: float
125	    unit: str
126	    source_tier: str
127	    source_url: str
128	    context_snippet: str
129	    endpoint_phrase: str
130	
131	
132	@dataclass(frozen=True)
133	class ContradictionCluster:
134	    """A tier-labeled disagreement cluster — extended with severity + action."""
135	
136	    cluster_id: int
137	    subject: str
138	    predicate: str
139	    severity: str
140	    absolute_difference: float
141	    relative_difference: float
142	    recommended_action: str
143	    claims: tuple[ContradictionClaim, ...]
144	
145	
146	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
147	# Frame coverage
148	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
149	
150	
151	@dataclass(frozen=True)
152	class RetrievalAttempt:
153	    """One retrieval attempt against an external source — frozen leaf."""
154	
155	    attempt_index: int
156	    source: str
157	    url: str
158	    outcome: str
159	    http_status: int | None
160	
161	
162	@dataclass(frozen=True)
163	class FrameCoverageEntry:
164	    """One contract-slot entity in the frame coverage report.
165	
166	    Includes section / slot_id / subsection_title (back-link into report
167	    structure) and min_fields_for_completion / human_curated_provenance
168	    (operator-completion workflow inputs).
169	    """
170	
171	    entity_id: str
172	    entity_type: str
173	    section: str
174	    slot_id: str
175	    subsection_title: str
176	    status: str
177	    doi: str | None
178	    pmid: str | None
179	    failure_reason: str | None
180	    available_artifacts: tuple[str, ...]
181	    required_fields: tuple[str, ...]
182	    min_fields_for_completion: int
183	    provenance_class: str
184	    human_completion_eligible: bool
185	    human_curated_provenance: str | None
186	    is_pipeline_fault: bool
187	    retrieval_attempt_log: tuple[RetrievalAttempt, ...]
188	
189	
190	@dataclass(frozen=True)
191	class FrameCoverageReport:
192	    """Frame coverage for the whole run.
193	
194	    `semantics_warning` preserves the V30 disclaimer that current
195	    `frame_coverage_report` reflects retrieval success, NOT verified-report
196	    coverage. Inspector view 3 must surface this.
197	    """
198	
199	    pass_count: int
200	    partial_count: int
201	    frame_gap_count: int
202	    pipeline_fault_count: int
203	    total_entities: int
204	    total_slots: int
205	    research_question: str
206	    schema_version: str
207	    semantics_warning: str | None
208	    entries: tuple[FrameCoverageEntry, ...]
209	
210	
211	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
212	# Model & rule provenance (Codex M-1 v2 review fix: View 4 prerequisites)
213	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
214	
215	
216	@dataclass(frozen=True)
217	class RuleCheck:
218	    """One pre-commit rule check from evaluator_rule_checks.json."""
219	
220	    item_id: str
221	    name: str
222	    passed: bool
223	    details: str
224	
225	
226	@dataclass(frozen=True)
227	class ModelProvenance:
228	    """Model and rule-check provenance for the Methods + Provenance Bundle.
229	
230	    Sources:
231	      - evaluator_rule_checks.json: generator_family/model, evaluator_family/model,

496	def _read_json(path: Path) -> Any:
497	    if not path.exists():
498	        raise FileNotFoundError(f"Required artifact missing: {path}")
499	    with path.open("r", encoding="utf-8") as f:

509	def _require_keys(d: Mapping[str, Any], keys: tuple[str, ...], where: str) -> None:
510	    missing = [k for k in keys if k not in d]
511	    if missing:
512	        raise AuditIRSchemaError(
513	            f"{where}: required keys missing: {missing}"
514	        )

517	def _parse_bibliography(raw: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> tuple[BibliographyEntry, ...]:
518	    if not isinstance(raw, list):
519	        raise AuditIRSchemaError("bibliography.json: expected a list")
520	    out = []
521	    for i, entry in enumerate(raw):
522	        _require_keys(entry, ("num", "evidence_id"), f"bibliography[{i}]")
523	        out.append(
524	            BibliographyEntry(
525	                num=int(entry["num"]),
526	                evidence_id=str(entry["evidence_id"]),
527	                statement=str(entry.get("statement", "")),
528	                tier=str(entry.get("tier", "UNKNOWN")),
529	                url=str(entry.get("url", "")),
530	            )
531	        )
532	    return tuple(out)

536	    raw: Mapping[str, Any], where: str
537	) -> ContradictionClaim:
538	    _require_keys(raw, ("evidence_id", "predicate", "value"), where)
539	    return ContradictionClaim(
540	        evidence_id=str(raw["evidence_id"]),
541	        subject=str(raw.get("subject", "")),
542	        predicate=str(raw["predicate"]),
543	        arm=str(raw.get("arm", "")),
544	        dose=str(raw.get("dose", "")),
545	        value=float(raw["value"]),
546	        unit=str(raw.get("unit", "")),
547	        source_tier=str(raw.get("source_tier", "UNKNOWN")),
548	        source_url=str(raw.get("source_url", "")),

554	def _parse_contradictions(
555	    raw: list[dict[str, Any]],
556	) -> tuple[ContradictionCluster, ...]:
557	    if not isinstance(raw, list):
558	        raise AuditIRSchemaError("contradictions.json: expected a list")
559	    clusters = []
560	    for idx, raw_cluster in enumerate(raw):
561	        where = f"contradictions[{idx}]"
562	        _require_keys(raw_cluster, ("predicate", "claims"), where)
563	        claims_raw = raw_cluster["claims"]
564	        if not isinstance(claims_raw, list) or len(claims_raw) < 2:
565	            raise AuditIRSchemaError(
566	                f"{where}: 'claims' must be a list of >=2 entries"
567	            )
568	        claims = tuple(
569	            _parse_contradiction_claim(c, f"{where}.claims[{j}]")
570	            for j, c in enumerate(claims_raw)
571	        )
572	        clusters.append(
573	            ContradictionCluster(
574	                cluster_id=idx,
575	                subject=str(raw_cluster.get("subject", "")),
576	                predicate=str(raw_cluster["predicate"]),
577	                severity=str(raw_cluster.get("severity", "unknown")),
578	                absolute_difference=float(raw_cluster.get("absolute_difference", 0.0)),
579	                relative_difference=float(raw_cluster.get("relative_difference", 0.0)),
580	                recommended_action=str(raw_cluster.get("recommended_action", "")),
581	                claims=claims,
582	            )
583	        )
584	    return tuple(clusters)

752	def _parse_completeness_percent(raw: Any) -> float:
753	    """Compute completeness as a percentage, 0.0–100.0.
754	
755	    V30 manifests use either:
756	      - {covered_fraction: 0.0–1.0}
757	      - {total_covered: int, total_applicable: int}
758	      - older keys: {covered_topics, total_topics} or {covered, total}
759	
760	    Codex M-1 review caught that the old code looked for keys that don't
761	    exist in the V30 schema, returning 0.0 for a 7/7 manifest.
762	    """
763	    if not isinstance(raw, Mapping):
764	        return 0.0
765	    if "covered_fraction" in raw:
766	        try:
767	            return float(raw["covered_fraction"]) * 100.0
768	        except (TypeError, ValueError):
769	            pass
770	    for cov_key, tot_key in (
771	        ("total_covered", "total_applicable"),
772	        ("covered_topics", "total_topics"),
773	        ("covered", "total"),
774	    ):
775	        if cov_key in raw and tot_key in raw:
776	            try:
777	                cov = float(raw[cov_key])
778	                tot = float(raw[tot_key])
779	                if tot > 0:
780	                    return (cov / tot) * 100.0
781	            except (TypeError, ValueError):
782	                continue
783	    return 0.0
784	
785	
786	def _parse_completeness_state(raw: Any) -> str:
787	    """FX-10 (I-ready-017): the completeness 3VL state for AuditIR consumers.
788	
789	    Prefer the explicit manifest ``completeness_state`` field; fall back to inferring
790	    from ``total_applicable`` for pre-FX-10 manifests so a consumer never reads a vacuous
791	    ``covered_fraction=1.0`` (no checklist applied) as a measured 100%.
792	    """
793	    if isinstance(raw, Mapping):
794	        state = raw.get("completeness_state")
795	        if isinstance(state, str) and state:
796	            return state
797	        if "total_applicable" in raw:
798	            try:
799	                if int(raw["total_applicable"]) == 0:
800	                    return "not_applicable"
801	            except (TypeError, ValueError):
802	                pass
803	    return "measured"

879	            for i, s in enumerate(kept_raw)

883	            for i, s in enumerate(dropped_raw)
884	        )

891	                dropped_due_to_failure=int(sec.get("dropped_due_to_failure", 0)),

893	            )
894	        )
895	    totals = raw.get("totals", {}) or {}
896	    drop_counts = raw.get("drop_reason_counts", {}) or {}
897	    return VerifiedReport(
898	        sections=tuple(sections),

900	        sentences_dropped=int(totals.get("sentences_dropped", 0)),

920	    """Codex M-1 v2 review fix: load model/version provenance from runtime artifacts.

924	    provenance silently zero-filling the missing half violates audit-grade

927	    if eval_rules_raw is None and judge_raw is None:
928	        return None

930	        present = "evaluator_rule_checks.json" if eval_rules_raw else "judge_output.json"
931	        missing = "judge_output.json" if eval_rules_raw else "evaluator_rule_checks.json"

934	            f"Both must be present together or both absent."

937	        _parse_rule_check(rc) for rc in eval_rules_raw.get("rule_checks", [])

944	        judge_model=str(judge_raw.get("model", "")),

946	        judge_input_tokens=int(judge_raw.get("input_tokens", 0)),
947	        judge_output_tokens=int(judge_raw.get("output_tokens", 0)),
948	        contradictions_disclosed=int(eval_rules_raw.get("contradictions_disclosed", 0)),
949	        contradictions_missing=tuple(
950	            str(c) for c in eval_rules_raw.get("contradictions_missing", [])
951	        ),

983	    """Read a JSON file but return None if it doesn't exist (vs failing loud)."""
984	    if not path.exists():

986	    with path.open("r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
987	        return json.load(f)

995	def load_audit_ir(artifact_dir: Path | str) -> AuditIR:
996	    """Load a V30 Phase-2 run artifact directory into a canonical AuditIR.
997	
998	    Required files in artifact_dir:
999	        manifest.json
1000	        report.md
1001	        bibliography.json
1002	        contradictions.json
1003	        verification_details.json
1004	
1005	    Raises
1006	    ------
1007	    NotADirectoryError
1008	        If `artifact_dir` is not an existing directory.
1009	    FileNotFoundError
1010	        If any required file is missing.
1011	    AuditIRSchemaError
1012	        If any required schema block is missing or malformed. The IR fails
1013	        loud at load-time per audit-grade discipline; renderers should not
1014	        have to defensively fill in missing data.
1015	    """
1016	    artifact_dir = Path(artifact_dir)
1017	    if not artifact_dir.is_dir():
1018	        raise NotADirectoryError(f"artifact_dir is not a directory: {artifact_dir}")
1019	
1020	    manifest_raw = _read_json(artifact_dir / "manifest.json")
1021	    if not isinstance(manifest_raw, Mapping):
1022	        raise AuditIRSchemaError("manifest.json: top-level must be a JSON object")
1023	    report_md = _read_text(artifact_dir / "report.md")
1024	    bibliography_raw = _read_json(artifact_dir / "bibliography.json")
1025	    contradictions_raw = _read_json(artifact_dir / "contradictions.json")
1026	    verification_raw = _read_json(artifact_dir / "verification_details.json")
1027	
1028	    # Optional provenance files — present in V30 Phase-2 runs, absent in legacy.
1029	    eval_rules_raw = _read_optional_json(artifact_dir / "evaluator_rule_checks.json")
1030	    judge_raw = _read_optional_json(artifact_dir / "judge_output.json")
1031	    if judge_raw is None:
1032	        # I-modref-004 (#530): legacy artifact-name fallback — historical
1033	        # runs wrote qwen_judge_output.json; keep loading them.
1034	        judge_raw = _read_optional_json(artifact_dir / "qwen_judge_output.json")
1035	    protocol_raw = _read_optional_json(artifact_dir / "protocol.json")
1036	    corpus_approval_raw = _read_optional_json(artifact_dir / "corpus_approval.json")
1037	
1038	    manifest = _parse_manifest(manifest_raw)
1039	    bibliography = _parse_bibliography(bibliography_raw)
1040	    contradictions = _parse_contradictions(contradictions_raw)
1041	    frame_coverage = _parse_frame_coverage(
1042	        manifest_raw.get("frame_coverage_report"),
1043	        manifest.v30_warnings,
1044	    )
1045	    tier_mix = _parse_tier_mix(manifest_raw.get("corpus"))
1046	    verified_report = _parse_verified_report(verification_raw)
1047	    model_provenance = _parse_model_provenance(eval_rules_raw, judge_raw)
1048	    protocol = _parse_protocol(protocol_raw)
1049	    adequacy = _parse_adequacy(manifest_raw.get("adequacy"))
1050	    corpus_approval = _parse_corpus_approval(corpus_approval_raw)
1051	
1052	    return AuditIR(
1053	        ir_schema_version=IR_SCHEMA_VERSION,
1054	        run_id=manifest.run_id,
1055	        artifact_dir=artifact_dir,
1056	        report_md=report_md,
1057	        manifest=manifest,
1058	        bibliography=bibliography,
1059	        contradictions=contradictions,
1060	        frame_coverage=frame_coverage,
1061	        tier_mix=tier_mix,
1062	        verified_report=verified_report,
1063	        model_provenance=model_provenance,
1064	        protocol=protocol,
1065	        adequacy=adequacy,
1066	        corpus_approval=corpus_approval,
1067	    )

347	def _record_scope_gate_decision(
348	    question: str,
349	    routing_result: object,
350	    *,
351	    workspace_id: str | None,
352	) -> None:
353	    """Best-effort scope-gate decision record.
354	
355	    Failure to write is logged but does NOT raise — telemetry must
356	    never gate the actual scope-gate decision. PG_RECORD_DECISIONS=0
357	    disables the write path.
358	
359	    `workspace_id` is the auth caller's org_id (one record per
360	    org's scope-gate calls). Anonymous calls (workspace_id=None)
361	    skip telemetry silently.
362	    """
363	    if os.environ.get("PG_RECORD_DECISIONS", "1") == "0":
364	        return
365	    if not workspace_id:
366	        return
367	    try:
368	        store = _get_decision_store()
369	        store.record_decision(
370	            workspace_id=workspace_id,
371	            decision_kind=DecisionKind.SCOPE_GATE,
372	            query=question,
373	            proposed_payload={
374	                "verdict": getattr(
375	                    getattr(routing_result, "verdict", None), "value", None,
376	                ),
377	                "template_id": getattr(routing_result, "template_id", None),
378	            },
379	            proposed_confidence=float(
380	                getattr(routing_result, "confidence", 0.0) or 0.0
381	            ),
382	        )
383	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — intentional broad
384	        _INT_0A_LOGGER.warning(
385	            "scope-gate decision telemetry failed (workspace_id=%s): %s",
386	            workspace_id, exc,
387	        )

623	@router.get("/api/inspector/runs/{slug}/audit-bundle.zip")
624	async def get_audit_bundle(slug: str):
625	    """Return a procurement-grade audit bundle as a zip file.
626	
627	    The bundle contains the canonical V30 artifact files + INDEX.txt
628	    (human-readable header with run identity, model provenance, gate
629	    decisions) + MANIFEST.SHA256 (per-file digests for tamper-evidence).
630	
631	    Phase A: builds the ZIP in-memory from the artifact directory.
632	    Phase B: pre-builds + caches per-run bundles in object storage with
633	    detached signatures.
634	
635	    Codex M-6 review fixes integrated:
636	    - Fails loud (500) if any canonical-required file is missing
637	    - INDEX.txt includes protocol_sha256, model IDs, gate decisions,
638	      file digests
639	    - MANIFEST.SHA256 carries SHA-256 of each bundled file
640	    - Adds run_log.txt + live_corpus_dump.json + cost_ledger.jsonl
641	      (optional, included if present)
642	    """
643	    import hashlib
644	    import io
645	    import json
646	    import zipfile
647	
648	    from fastapi.responses import StreamingResponse
649	
650	    summary = find_run_by_slug(slug)
651	    if summary is None:
652	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f"Unknown run slug: {slug}")
653	    artifact_dir = summary.artifact_dir
654	
655	    # Canonical-required files: must be present for the bundle to be valid.
656	    # Codex M-6 fix: fail loud if any are missing.
657	    required_files = [
658	        "report.md",
659	        "manifest.json",
660	        "bibliography.json",
661	        "contradictions.json",
662	        "verification_details.json",
663	    ]
664	    # Optional canonical artifacts: included if present.
665	    optional_files = [
666	        "protocol.json",
667	        "evaluator_rule_checks.json",
668	        "judge_output.json",
669	        "qwen_judge_output.json",  # I-modref-004 (#530): legacy name — historical runs still surface
670	        "completeness.json",
671	        "corpus_adequacy.json",
672	        "corpus_approval.json",
673	        "human_gap_tasks.json",
674	        # Codex M-6 fix: add scope SHA + stage trail + corpus provenance.
675	        "run_log.txt",
676	        "live_corpus_dump.json",
677	        "cost_ledger.jsonl",
678	    ]
679	
680	    missing_required = [f for f in required_files if not (artifact_dir / f).exists()]
681	    if missing_required:
682	        raise HTTPException(
683	            status_code=500,
684	            detail=(
685	                f"Audit bundle cannot be built: required artifact files "
686	                f"missing for run {summary.run_id}: {missing_required}"
687	            ),
688	        )
689	
690	    # Pull additional provenance fields from manifest for the INDEX header.
691	    try:
692	        with (artifact_dir / "manifest.json").open("r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
693	            manifest = json.load(f)
694	    except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError) as exc:
695	        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=f"manifest.json unreadable: {exc}")
696	
697	    eval_rules = {}
698	    eval_rules_path = artifact_dir / "evaluator_rule_checks.json"
699	    if eval_rules_path.exists():
700	        try:
701	            with eval_rules_path.open("r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
702	                eval_rules = json.load(f)
703	        except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError):
704	            eval_rules = {}
705	
706	    eval_gate = manifest.get("evaluator_gate", {})
707	    if not isinstance(eval_gate, dict):
708	        eval_gate = {"gate_class": str(eval_gate)}
709	    adequacy = manifest.get("adequacy", {}) or {}
710	    corpus = manifest.get("corpus", {}) or {}
711	
712	    bundle_files = required_files + optional_files
713	
714	    # Codex M-16: also embed the AuditIR JSON projection so the
715	    # bundle is round-trippable through `load_audit_ir` without
716	    # re-running the loader on the raw artifact files. Without
717	    # this, downstream consumers (e.g. M-23 review queue, M-25
718	    # private-corpus sync) have to re-parse manifest+contradictions
719	    # +verification+bibliography just to render the same Inspector
720	    # view the bundle is meant to capture.
721	    auditir_json = None
722	    try:
723	        from src.polaris_graph.audit_ir.loader import load_audit_ir
724	        from src.polaris_graph.audit_ir.serializer import to_json_dict
725	        ir = load_audit_ir(artifact_dir)
726	        auditir_json = json.dumps(to_json_dict(ir), indent=2, sort_keys=True)
727	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — best-effort
728	        # Don't fail the whole bundle if AuditIR projection has
729	        # issues; the raw artifact files are still complete.
730	        # M-16 review may want this stricter — change to raise.
731	        auditir_json = None
732	
733	    buf = io.BytesIO()
734	    with zipfile.ZipFile(buf, "w", zipfile.ZIP_DEFLATED) as zf:
735	        # First pass: compute digests + write each file
736	        digests: list[tuple[str, int, str]] = []
737	        for fname in bundle_files:
738	            path = artifact_dir / fname
739	            if not path.exists():
740	                continue
741	            data = path.read_bytes()
742	            digest = hashlib.sha256(data).hexdigest()
743	            digests.append((fname, len(data), digest))
744	            zf.writestr(fname, data)
745	
746	        # Codex M-16: AuditIR JSON projection.
747	        if auditir_json is not None:
748	            data = auditir_json.encode("utf-8")
749	            digest = hashlib.sha256(data).hexdigest()
750	            digests.append(("audit_ir.json", len(data), digest))
751	            zf.writestr("audit_ir.json", data)
752	
753	        # MANIFEST.SHA256 — per-file digests for tamper-evidence.
754	        sha_lines = [f"{digest}  {fname}" for (fname, _size, digest) in digests]
755	        zf.writestr("MANIFEST.SHA256", "\n".join(sha_lines) + "\n")
756	
757	        # INDEX.txt — comprehensive procurement header.
758	        index_lines = [
759	            "POLARIS V30 Phase-2 Audit Bundle",
760	            "================================",
761	            "",
762	            "RUN IDENTITY",
763	            "------------",
764	            f"Run slug:           {summary.slug}",
765	            f"Run ID:             {summary.run_id}",
766	            f"Protocol SHA-256:   {manifest.get('protocol_sha256', '—')}",
767	            f"Status:             {summary.status}",
768	            f"Created at (ISO):   {summary.created_at_iso or '—'}",
769	            f"Word count:         {summary.word_count}",
770	            f"Cost (USD):         {summary.cost_usd:.6f}",
771	            f"Contradictions:     {summary.contradictions_found}",
772	            f"Release allowed:    {summary.release_allowed}",
773	            "",
774	            "MODEL PROVENANCE",
775	            "----------------",
776	            f"Generator family:   {eval_rules.get('generator_family', '—')}",
777	            f"Generator model:    {eval_rules.get('generator_model', '—')}",
778	            f"Evaluator family:   {eval_rules.get('evaluator_family', '—')}",
779	            f"Evaluator model:    {eval_rules.get('evaluator_model', '—')}",
780	            "",
781	            "GATE DECISIONS",
782	            "--------------",
783	            f"Adequacy:           decision={adequacy.get('decision', '—')}, "
784	            f"findings_ok={adequacy.get('findings_ok', '—')}/{adequacy.get('findings_total', '—')}",
785	            f"Corpus approved:    {corpus.get('approved', '—')} "
786	            f"(material_deviation={corpus.get('material_deviation', '—')}, count={corpus.get('count', '—')})",
787	            f"Evaluator gate:     class={eval_gate.get('gate_class', '—')}, "
788	            f"release_allowed={eval_gate.get('release_allowed', '—')}",
789	            f"Reasons:            {', '.join(str(r) for r in eval_gate.get('reasons', [])) or '—'}",
790	            f"Rule blockers:      {', '.join(str(r) for r in eval_gate.get('rule_blockers', [])) or '—'}",
791	            "",
792	            "BUNDLE FILES + DIGESTS (SHA-256)",
793	            "--------------------------------",
794	        ]
795	        for fname, size, digest in digests:
796	            index_lines.append(f"  {digest}  {size:>10} bytes  {fname}")
797	        index_lines.append("")
798	        index_lines.append(
799	            "Verify: sha256sum -c MANIFEST.SHA256"
800	        )
801	        index_lines.append("")
802	        index_lines.append(
803	            "This bundle is the procurement-grade reproducibility artifact. "
804	            "Every claim in report.md is bound to an evidence_id in "
805	            "verification_details.json; bibliography.json maps [N] -> "
806	            "evidence_id; contradictions.json declares disagreements; "
807	            "manifest.json carries gates, costs, and the protocol_sha256."
808	        )
809	        zf.writestr("INDEX.txt", "\n".join(index_lines))
810	
811	    buf.seek(0)
812	    headers = {
813	        "Content-Disposition": (
814	            f'attachment; filename="polaris-audit-bundle-{summary.slug}.zip"'
815	        ),
816	    }
817	    return StreamingResponse(
818	        iter([buf.getvalue()]),
819	        media_type="application/zip",
820	        headers=headers,
821	    )

1141	class _PrivateCorpusSourceCreateRequest(BaseModel):
1142	    """Codex round-1 MEDIUM fix (v2): enforce extra='forbid' so
1143	    callers cannot pass a `connector` field at all (v1 used
1144	    Pydantic default which silently dropped extras, leaving room
1145	    for confusion). v2 explicitly rejects unknown fields with 422.

1148	    workspace_id: str = Field(..., min_length=1)
1149	    name: str = Field(..., min_length=1)
1150	    external_uri: str = Field(
1151	        ..., min_length=1,
1152	        description=(
1153	            "Google Drive folder ID (e.g. '1AbC...'). "
1154	            "URLs and other non-folder-ID values are rejected."
1155	        ),
1156	    )
1157	    credential_ref: str = Field(
1158	        ..., min_length=1,

1166	# Codex round-1 MEDIUM fix (v2): validate external_uri shape.
1167	# Drive folder IDs are 28-44 chars of [A-Za-z0-9_-], no slashes
1168	# or dots. Reject URLs (anything containing '://', '.', or '/')
1169	# at endpoint level — substrate only checks non-empty.
1170	import re as _re  # local alias to avoid shadowing top-level imports
1171	_DRIVE_FOLDER_ID_RE = _re.compile(r"^[A-Za-z0-9_-]{20,80}$")

1174	def _validate_drive_folder_id(uri: str) -> None:
1175	    """Raise HTTPException(400) if uri doesn't look like a Drive
1176	    folder ID. Per FINAL_PLAN narrow scope, we want callers to
1177	    fail fast at the endpoint instead of mislabeled SharePoint
1178	    URLs landing as connector=google_drive."""
1179	    if not _DRIVE_FOLDER_ID_RE.match(uri):
1180	        raise HTTPException(
1181	            status_code=400,
1182	            detail=(
1183	                f"external_uri {uri!r} is not a valid Google Drive "
1184	                "folder ID; expected 20-80 chars of [A-Za-z0-9_-] "
1185	                "(e.g. '1AbC...DEF'). URLs and other shapes are not "
1186	                "accepted at this endpoint."
1187	            ),
1188	        )

1647	    / FRAME_COVERAGE / CONTRADICTION_QUEUE / VERIFIED_CLAIM /
1648	    SYNTHESIS_COMPLETE. Each kind appears at most once in the
1649	    snapshot — the most recent emission wins.

1651	    Returns 404 if the job_id is unknown to the queue.

1655	    )

1657	    if queue.get(job_id) is None:
1658	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f"unknown job_id={job_id}")
1659	    bus = get_surface_bus()
1660	    events = bus.latest_snapshot(job_id)

1667	@router.get("/api/inspector/jobs/{job_id}/stream")
1668	async def stream_job_surfaces(
1669	    job_id: str,
1670	    caller: Caller = Depends(require_job_viewer),
1671	) -> StreamingResponse:
1672	    """Server-Sent Events stream of progressive surfaces for this
1673	    job. Snapshot replays first (so a late-joining client gets the
1674	    full state), then live tail.
1675	
1676	    Stream terminates when the bus prunes the job_id (worker has
1677	    transitioned to a terminal status).
1678	    """
1679	    import json as _json
1680	    from src.polaris_graph.audit_ir.progress_surfaces import (
1681	        get_surface_bus,
1682	    )
1683	    queue = get_job_queue()
1684	    if queue.get(job_id) is None:
1685	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f"unknown job_id={job_id}")
1686	    bus = get_surface_bus()
1687	
1688	    async def _event_stream():
1689	        # Codex M-13 v2 review fix: atomic subscribe + snapshot
1690	        # capture so events emitted between subscribe and
1691	        # snapshot-read aren't double-delivered. Also returns
1692	        # is_terminal=True if the worker already pruned the
1693	        # job_id; in that case there will never be a sentinel
1694	        # so we replay the snapshot and emit `event: end`
1695	        # immediately rather than hanging on the empty queue.
1696	        sub_q, snapshot, terminal = bus.subscribe_with_snapshot(job_id)
1697	        try:
1698	            for event in snapshot:
1699	                yield f"data: {_json.dumps(event.to_dict())}\n\n"
1700	            if terminal:
1701	                yield "event: end\ndata: {}\n\n"
1702	                return
1703	            while True:
1704	                event = await sub_q.get()
1705	                if event is None:
1706	                    # Sentinel from prune() — job is terminal.
1707	                    yield "event: end\ndata: {}\n\n"
1708	                    return
1709	                yield f"data: {_json.dumps(event.to_dict())}\n\n"
1710	        finally:
1711	            bus.unsubscribe(job_id, sub_q)
1712	
1713	    return StreamingResponse(
1714	        _event_stream(),
1715	        media_type="text/event-stream",
1716	        headers={
1717	            "Cache-Control": "no-cache",
1718	            "X-Accel-Buffering": "no",  # disable proxy buffering
1719	        },
1720	    )

1897	def _sanitize_upload_filename(raw: str | None) -> str:
1898	    """Codex M-11 review fix: reduce a multipart filename to a safe
1899	    basename. Defends against:

1900	      - path traversal: "../../etc/passwd"
1901	      - absolute paths: "/etc/passwd", "C:/abs.txt", "C:\\abs.txt"
1902	      - nested paths: "subdir/name.txt"
1903	      - Unicode separators / NUL bytes
1904	      - empty / dot-only names
1905	
1906	    Phase B always rewrites the on-disk path to
1907	    `<root>/<workspace_id>/<upload_id>/<sanitized>` so even a
1908	    malicious sanitized basename cannot escape the upload-specific
1909	    directory.
1910	    """
1911	    if not raw:
1912	        return "upload"
1913	    # Strip NUL bytes (Windows can choke on these); strip whitespace.
1914	    # FastAPI's multipart parser URL-encodes literal NUL to "%00" by
1915	    # the time we see it, so strip both forms.
1916	    name = raw.replace("\x00", "").replace("%00", "").strip()
1917	    # Take the basename via both path separators so paths constructed
1918	    # on either OS get reduced. Apply repeatedly to defend against
1919	    # encoded "../" sequences.
1920	    while True:
1921	        prev = name
1922	        # Strip leading drive letters ("C:") and root markers.
1923	        if len(name) >= 2 and name[1] == ":":
1924	            name = name[2:]
1925	        # Strip both separators in priority order.
1926	        for sep in ("/", "\\"):
1927	            if sep in name:
1928	                name = name.rsplit(sep, 1)[-1]
1929	        if name == prev:
1930	            break
1931	    # Reject parent-dir / current-dir markers.
1932	    if name in {"", ".", ".."}:
1933	        return "upload"
1934	    # Reject leading dot-segment (still hides the file but cleaner UX).
1935	    while name.startswith("../") or name.startswith("..\\"):
1936	        name = name[3:]
1937	    # Final basename via os.path.basename for parity.
1938	    import os as _os
1939	    name = _os.path.basename(name)
1940	    if not name or name in {".", ".."}:
1941	        return "upload"
1942	    return name
1943

1945	@router.post("/api/inspector/workspaces/{workspace_id}/uploads")
1946	async def upload_to_workspace(
1947	    workspace_id: str,
1948	    file: UploadFile = File(...),
1949	    caller: Caller = Depends(require_workspace_member),
1950	) -> dict:
1951	    """Upload a file to the workspace. Phase B parses text uploads
1952	    synchronously; PDF and other formats land as `pending` /
1953	    `failed` per the parser's `can_handle` decision.
1954	
1955	    Bounded enforcement happens BEFORE the file bytes are written
1956	    so a rejected upload doesn't leave orphaned files on disk.
1957	
1958	    Codex M-11 review fix: filename is sanitized to a basename
1959	    before any disk write, and the on-disk path is constructed
1960	    inside the per-upload directory and verified to be within
1961	    the workspace root.
1962	    """
1963	    from src.polaris_graph.audit_ir.parser_runner import (
1964	        ParserError,
1965	        select_parser,
1966	        parse_result_to_chunk_dicts,
1967	    )
1968	    from src.polaris_graph.audit_ir.workspace_store import (
1969	        BoundedError,
1970	        WorkspaceStateError,
1971	        upload_to_dict,
1972	    )
1973	
1974	    store = get_workspace_store()
1975	    if store.get_workspace(workspace_id) is None:
1976	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f"unknown workspace: {workspace_id}")
1977	
1978	    filename = _sanitize_upload_filename(file.filename)
1979	    content_type = file.content_type
1980	    payload = await file.read()
1981	    size_bytes = len(payload)
1982	
1983	    # Reserve the upload row first (with a tentative storage_path)
1984	    # so bounded enforcement happens before we touch the disk.
1985	    files_root = _get_workspace_files_root()
1986	    workspace_dir = files_root / workspace_id
1987	    workspace_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
1988	    try:
1989	        upload = store.upload_file(
1990	            workspace_id=workspace_id, filename=filename,
1991	            content_type=content_type, size_bytes=size_bytes,
1992	            storage_path="",  # filled in below
1993	        )
1994	    except BoundedError as exc:
1995	        raise HTTPException(status_code=409, detail=str(exc))
1996	    except WorkspaceStateError as exc:
1997	        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail=str(exc))
1998	
1999	    upload_dir = workspace_dir / upload.upload_id
2000	    upload_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
2001	    storage_path = upload_dir / filename
2002	
2003	    # Defense in depth: resolve and verify the path is inside the
2004	    # workspace root. Sanitization above should already prevent
2005	    # escape, but a corrupted filesystem (symlink) could still
2006	    # redirect — refuse if so.
2007	    resolved = storage_path.resolve()
2008	    workspace_root_resolved = files_root.resolve()
2009	    try:
2010	        resolved.relative_to(workspace_root_resolved)
2011	    except ValueError:
2012	        # Soft-delete the reserved row so the cap recovers.
2013	        store.soft_delete_upload(upload.upload_id)
2014	        raise HTTPException(
2015	            status_code=400,
2016	            detail="upload path escapes workspace root; refused",
2017	        )
2018	
2019	    storage_path.write_bytes(payload)
2020	    # Codex M-11 v2 review fix: if a concurrent soft-delete races
2021	    # past our reservation, update_storage_path now raises rather
2022	    # than silently no-opping. Clean up the bytes on disk so we
2023	    # don't leak orphaned files for a deleted upload.
2024	    try:
2025	        store.update_storage_path(upload.upload_id, str(storage_path))
2026	    except WorkspaceStateError as exc:
2027	        try:
2028	            storage_path.unlink(missing_ok=True)
2029	        except OSError:
2030	            pass
2031	        raise HTTPException(status_code=409, detail=str(exc))
2032	
2033	    # Synchronously parse if a parser claims it. Phase C M-11.5
2034	    # will switch this to async via JobQueue for slow extractors.
2035	    parser = select_parser(filename, content_type)
2036	    if parser is None:
2037	        # No parser → leave status='pending'; operator decides.
2038	        refreshed = store.get_upload(upload.upload_id)
2039	        return upload_to_dict(refreshed)
2040	
2041	    store.transition_parser_status(upload.upload_id, "parsing")
2042	    try:
2043	        result = parser.parse(upload.upload_id, storage_path)
2044	    except ParserError as exc:
2045	        failed = store.transition_parser_status(
2046	            upload.upload_id, "failed", parser_error=str(exc),
2047	        )
2048	        return upload_to_dict(failed)
2049	
2050	    chunk_dicts = parse_result_to_chunk_dicts(result)
2051	    if chunk_dicts:
2052	        store.insert_chunks(upload.upload_id, chunk_dicts)
2053	    parsed = store.transition_parser_status(upload.upload_id, "parsed")
2054	    return upload_to_dict(parsed)

2950	def _percentile_ns(values: list[int], q: float) -> int:
2951	    """Nearest-rank percentile.
2952	
2953	    v2 R1 P1 fix: v1 used `int(q * len(s))` which selects the
2954	    NEXT-higher element instead of the correct sorted-list
2955	    index. Codex repro:
2956	      _percentile_ns([10, 20], 0.50) returned 20 (should be 10)
2957	      _percentile_ns(range(1, 101), 0.95) returned 96 (should be 95)
2958	      _percentile_ns(range(1, 101), 0.99) returned 100 (should be 99)
2959	    Correct nearest-rank formula: idx = ceil(q * n) - 1
2960	    (1-indexed → 0-indexed conversion).
2961	    """
2962	    if not values:
2963	        return 0
2964	    s = sorted(values)
2965	    n = len(s)
2966	    import math
2967	    idx = max(0, min(n - 1, math.ceil(q * n) - 1))
2968	    return s[idx]
